fields.name: Spoffin Off
fields.description: The Spoffin Spin-Off is a one-day street arts festival featuring Dutch acts and concluding with a fire performance. It takes place at the Lieve Vrouwekerkhof and nearby squares, with free performances and optional donations.
fields.comments: already contacted by emailapplication phase: 1 january and 28 february 2023 -> https://spoffin.eu/artists/off/.

fields.name: Aurrilac Festival
fields.description: The Festival International de Théâtre de Rue d'Aurillac, also known as the Aurillac Street Theatre Festival, is an annual event celebrating street theatre arts. The 38th edition will take place in 2025.

fields.name: Avignon Festival
fields.description: The 79th edition of the Avignon Festival, a major international performing arts event, will take place from July 4 to 27, 2025. The festival features a diverse program including theatre, dance, and multidisciplinary performances, with a special focus on Arabic as the invited language. The event includes professional development programs such as the European Theatre Academy (July 7–11, 2025), masterclasses at the Ecole internationale de théâtre Jacques Lecoq, and workshops for emerging artists. The festival is divided into the main 'IN' festival (July 4–27) and the 'OFF' festival (July 5–26).

fields.name: Young Stage
fields.description: The Young Stage International Circus Festival Basel is the largest circus festival in Switzerland and one of the most significant worldwide. It serves as a platform for the world's best young professional circus artists to showcase their talent and compete for awards and engagements. The festival features spectacular, poetic, funny, and surprising performances, with artists sharing their messages and life stories through their acts.

fields.name: Atc Festival
fields.description: The 22nd International ACT Festival for Emerging Performing Artists in Bilbao and Barakaldo, featuring contemporary dance performances at venues like the Guggenheim Museum Bilbao, Sala BBK Aretoa, Kafe Antzokia, Teatro Campos, and Teatro Barakaldo Antzokia. Highlights include works by Korean duo GOMsisun and Lyon-based Cie MF | Maxime & Francesco.

fields.name: Antistatic Dance Festival
fields.description: The Antistatic International Festival for Contemporary Dance and Performance is an annual event held in Sofia, Bulgaria. Founded in 2008, it is the first festival of its kind in the city and is organized by the 'Brain Store Project' Foundation and 'Nomad Dance Academy Bulgaria' Association. The festival explores contemporary dance as a political act, focusing on themes of resistance through movement, from subtle gestures to radical choreographic expressions. The 18th edition in 2025 adopted the theme 'Signs of Resistance,' aiming to redefine possibilities in a world that often demands conformity.
fields.comments: sent two emails, both failed to deliver

fields.name: C!Caf Copenhagen Circus Arts Festival
fields.description: The Copenhagen Circus Arts Festival (C!CAF) is a biennale event presenting international, high-quality contemporary circus art. Organized by Kitt Johnson X-act, the festival explores the boundaries between circus and art, featuring performances, workshops, and artist talks under the 'DEEP DIVE ACTIVITIES' program.
fields.comments: next edition (c!caf vol > 4) confirmed for spring 2026. no application process details found on official sources; default contact email sourced from organizer’s main website (kitt johnson x-act). festival type updated to circus based on domain-specific terminology ('contemporary circus art').

fields.name: Dynamo Circus Festival
fields.description: Denmark's largest festival for contemporary circus, the DYNAMO Circus Festival 2025 marks its 6th edition with a four-day 'tour de force of wildness, poetry, humor, and magic.' The event features an international lineup of artists, music, and diverse performing arts experiences across over five stages, including two large circus tents at Odense harbor. Highlights include 'LOW COST PARADISE' by Cirque Pardi!, 'OPA FASHION' (a blend of contemporary circus and live music), and acts by Cirkus Tværs. The festival also offers circus films, an open stage for emerging and established artists, and aims to be a gathering point for artists, families, and audiences interested in contemporary circus.

fields.name: Dance In The City - Festival Dancing City
fields.description: The 19th International DANCE Festival München aims for artistic intensity and an expanded presence throughout the city of Munich, with 19 productions and 45 performances across 12 venues.

fields.name: Düsseldorf Festival Full Spin
fields.description: An international biennial physical theatre festival hosted in Essen and Münster, Germany, in collaboration with institutions such as the Folkwang University of the Arts. The 7th edition was held in 2024, with the next iteration expected in 2026.
fields.comments: festival has not been updated since a long time!

fields.name: Festival No Elephants - Freiburg Circus Days
fields.description: A festival showcasing contemporary circus at the E-WERK Freiburg, featuring acrobatics, object manipulation, dance, theater, music, and media.

fields.name: Neuland Festival
fields.description: The Neuland Festival includes various events such as the Neuland Winter-Varieté in December 2025, regional day festivals (OPEN Neuland Festivals) in multiple locations throughout the year, the DOK Neuland exhibition focusing on Extended Reality (XR), and the NEULAND Kammermusikfest Darmstadt featuring classical music concerts.
fields.town: Oldenburg, Schortens, Cuxhaven, Hoyerswerda, Angermünde, Lindstedt, Darmstadt, Aachen

fields.name: International Dance Fair Nrw
fields.description: FESTIVAL TANZ NRW 25 is a biannual dance festival organized by Tanzhaus NRW in Düsseldorf, presenting a diverse range of choreographic works from North Rhine-Westphalia. The festival features both emerging and established choreographers, aiming to create spaces for exchange and agency among artists, promoters, and visitors. The 2025 edition marks its anniversary and will open at Tanzhaus NRW.

fields.name: Edinburgh Festival Fringe
fields.description: The Edinburgh Festival Fringe is the world's largest open-access arts festival, featuring a diverse range of performances including comedy, theatre, music, dance, cabaret, circus, and more. The 2025 festival runs from July 28 to August 25, with the full program launching on June 3.

fields.name: Nous Nous Festival - Cirko Circus Center
fields.description: The OuNous Festival is a five-day event dedicated to physical comedy and contemporary circus arts, featuring both Finnish and international artists. The festival includes performances, demo nights, and circus-themed movie screenings. Previously known as the NouNous Festival, this edition introduces spoken-word performances alongside its signature wordless physical comedy. The program features acts such as 'Only Bones' by Marina Cherry, 'Hamish' by Mika Formunen, and 'The Letter' by Paolo Nani, as well as family-friendly shows like 'Incredible Michael Might and his Amazing Magic Show' by Piste Kollektiivi. Organized by the Kallo Collective at the Cirko Circus Center in Helsinki.

fields.name: X Dance Festival
fields.description: The X Dance Festival 2025, organized by Kaaos Company, is an international festival serving as a meeting point for disabled and non-disabled artists, educators, and allies shaping the future of dance. The 2025 theme emphasizes 'bold, collective movement' and aims to declare that inclusive dance will not be suppressed or erased. The festival features performances, immersive workshops, and discussions, with the detailed program expected in spring 2025.

fields.name: Bibasse
fields.description: The Bibasse juggling convention, an essential event for the French flow arts and juggling scene, offering five days of circus shows, concerts, workshops, fire jams, and juggling games.

fields.name: Biennale Internationale Des Arts De La Marionnette
fields.description: The 12th edition of the Biennale Internationale des Arts de la Marionnette (BIAM) showcases the diversity and creativity of contemporary puppetry. Organized by Le Mouffetard – Centre national de la Marionnette, this biennial event spans 15 cities in the Île-de-France region, featuring French and international artists presenting their recent creations across numerous venues. BIAM has been a significant fixture in the contemporary puppet scene since 2001, offering a comprehensive overview of current trends in puppetry arts.
fields.town: Paris And Île-De-France Region (15 Cities Including Paris, Pantin, Fontenay-Sous-Bois)

fields.name: Chalon Dans La Rue
fields.description: Chalon dans la Rue is an international street arts festival that transforms the city of Chalon-sur-Saône into an open-air stage. It features a diverse program including street theatre, dance, music, circus, puppetry, performances, and plastic installations. The festival attracts around 200,000 to 220,000 spectators annually, making it the second-largest street arts festival in France. The 2025 edition will focus on artistic diversity, societal engagement, and bold creations ranging from intimate to monumental, poetic to festive.

fields.name: Festival International Des Artistes Du Cirque
fields.description: The 24th edition of the Festival International des Artistes du Cirque de Saint-Paul-lès-Dax, one of the largest circus festivals in Europe. It features a variety of international artists presenting acts of acrobatics, magic, emotion, aerial performances, balance, juggling, visual poetry, and technical prowess.

fields.name: Festival Itinerant De Marionnettes
fields.description: The Festival Itinérant de Marionnettes (FIM) is a 17th-edition event celebrating the rich and magical universe of puppet arts, including puppetry, circus, dance, theater, and Kamishibai. Hosted across multiple locations in the Valenciennes Métropole region, the festival features diverse performances with a single ticket rate of 5 euros (free for children under 1 year). Reservations are mandatory due to limited seating.

fields.name: Marto Marionnettes And Objects Festival
fields.description: The 25th edition of the Marto Marionnettes and Objects Festival, dedicated to puppetry and object theatre arts. The event spans nine cities in the Hauts-de-Seine department, featuring 14 shows and installations. Highlights include live animation films, dystopian performances with music and acrobatics, and pieces for both adult and younger audiences. The festival also offers master classes, exhibitions, and artist encounters.
fields.town: Antony, Bagneux, Châtenay-Malabry, Châtillon, and others

fields.name: Meli Melo Festival
fields.description: The 25th edition of the Méli-Mélo Festival of puppets and animated forms, an itinerant event hosted across 11 communes near Bordeaux. The festival features 15 regional, national, and international companies offering over 50 performances. The inauguration takes place on February 4, 2025, at Halle polyvalente du Bouzet in Cestas.
fields.town: Canéjan, Cestas, Pessac, Martignas-Sur-Jalle, Saint Jean D'Illac, Beautiran, and others

fields.name: Mimesis
fields.description: No dedicated 'Mimesis France 2025' festival found. The term 'Mimesis' appears in unrelated contexts: a documentary festival in Boulder, Colorado (USA); a book signing event at Institut Lumière in October 2025; and a conference in Vilnius, Lithuania (October 2–4, 2025).

fields.name: Les nuits de Fourvière
fields.description: The 79th edition of the Nuits de Fourvière festival in Lyon, France, features a diverse program encompassing music, dance, magic, circus, and theater. Held at the ancient Roman theaters of Fourvière and across the Lyon metropolis, the 2025 lineup includes artists such as Tinariwen, Mulatu Astatke, Les Amazones d'Afrique, Anna Mouglalis, Fontaines D.C., Marc Rebillet, Feu! Chatterton, The Libertines, Max Richter, Beth Gibbons, Lamomali, Clara Luciani, Jean-Louis Aubert, Ben Mazué, Cypress Hill, Franz Ferdinand, Kraftwerk, Jorja Smith, Anoushka Shankar, and Chilly Gonzales. Special programming includes 'Samedis des Nuits' (a range of musical styles) and 'Petites Nuits' (for younger audiences).

fields.name: Marionnettissimo
fields.description: The 28th edition of Marionnettissimo, an international festival of puppetry and animated forms, featuring spectacles, workshops, festive evenings, and exhibitions for all ages. Highlights include an inauguration on November 20, a 'Mario'Night' with marionettic DJ sets on November 22, and the closing spectacle 'Trust Me for A While' by Plexus Polaire on November 23. Operates as a 'Scène conventionnée d'intérêt national' (National interest-accredited stage) for puppetry arts, presented in partnership with L'Escale – Ville de Tournefeuille.

fields.name: Occitanie Fait Son Cirque
fields.description: A festival showcasing the ingenuity and diversity of contemporary circus creation through emerging and renowned companies. It establishes a temporary circus village on the Île Piot in Avignon, featuring chapiteaus and performance spaces.

fields.name: Artisti In Piazza
fields.description: The Artisti in Piazza International Festival of Performing Arts is a street festival featuring circus, theater, music, and dance performances. The 2025 edition, themed 'Autopoiesi,' will showcase over 50 international companies with premieres and original productions.

fields.name: Bascherdeis
fields.description: The Bascherdeis International Festival of Street Artists transforms the historic center of Vernasca, Italy, into an open-air theater. This 20th-anniversary edition (2025) features over 20 stages with artists from around the world, including jugglers, acrobats, musicians, clowns, equilibrists, fire-eaters, and storytellers. The event also includes artisan stalls, local food and drink, and free admission. The historic center is closed to traffic, with shuttle services available from designated parking areas.

fields.name: Circum Navigando
fields.description: The 25th edition of the Circumnavigando International Festival of Circus Theatre, themed 'FRAGILE,' blends contemporary circus and theater performances across various genres and styles. Organized by Associazione Sarabanda Impresa Sociale, the festival features events at multiple venues in Genoa, including Palazzo Ducale, and serves as a national and international reference for innovative artistic forms since 2001.

fields.name: Ferrara Buskers Festival
fields.description: The Ferrara Buskers Festival is a renowned international street performance festival held in the historic center of Ferrara, Italy. The 2025 edition will feature over 250 artists from 19 countries, including first-time participants from Taiwan. The festival spans five days, with performances from 7:00 PM to midnight (5:00 PM to 9:00 PM on Sunday), alongside workshops, talks, and a tribute parade. A preview event will take place in Comacchio on August 24. Tickets are required for entry, with free admission for children under 13 and caregivers of people with disabilities. The festival also introduces the 'Gianna Nannini Award' and 'La Scuola Buskers' workshops in 2025.

fields.name: International Circus Festival Italy
fields.description: The 26th International Circus Festival of Italy is one of the world's leading circus festivals, featuring extraordinary artists from over 20 nations. The event includes competitive performances evaluated by an International Technical Jury and a Critics' Jury, along with collateral events such as a football match and an international mass. Hosted under a grand 42-meter diameter chapiteau in the weekly market area of Latina, specifically on Via Rossetti.

fields.name: Circus Days Kufstein
fields.description: The Circustage Kufstein (Circus Days Kufstein) is a modern circus arts festival featuring performances and workshops in acrobatics, juggling, and balance arts. The 2025 edition will be a certified 'Greenevent' and will take place at the Kultur Quartier Kufstein, with additional performances in nursing homes in Langkampfen and Ebbs.

fields.name: At.Tension
fields.description: The at.tension festival is a biennial interdisciplinary theatre festival held on a 100-hectare site in Lärz, featuring former airplane hangars, a landing strip, circus tents, camping areas, and a swimming lake. The 2026 edition, at.tension#11, is scheduled for September 3–6, 2026.

fields.name: Flying Carpet Festival
fields.description: The Flying Carpet Festival is the world's first mobile arts festival dedicated to children in conflict zones and underserved regions. It features music, circus, poetry, and performance, transforming public spaces into areas of wonder and joy. The 2025 edition is inspired by Carlo Collodi's 'Pinocchio,' focusing on themes of transformation and freedom.

fields.name: Freeman Festival
fields.description: The Freeman Festival focuses on contemporary circus and performance, celebrating artistic freedom, community, and connection. It brings together international artists and the local community through performances, workshops, and encounters.

fields.name: Nitra Theatre Festival
fields.description: The 34th International Theatre Festival Divadelná Nitra, themed 'Right(s) Here, Right(s) Now!', reflects on current socio-political issues, fundamental rights, and the value of free artistic expression. The 2025 edition features 14 productions from seven countries (Austria, Belarus, Czech Republic, Germany, Hungary, Russia, and Slovakia), including three world premieres. The festival also provides a platform for Belarusian and Russian artists affected by the war in Ukraine. Beyond performances, the program includes discussions, talks, concerts, workshops, and programs for younger audiences.

fields.name: Festspillene I Nord-Norge (Arctic Arts Festival)
fields.description: The Arctic Arts Festival (Festspillene i Nord-Norge) is an eight-day international arts festival dedicated to music, theater, dance, and visual arts, held annually during the summer solstice in June. The 2025 edition marks the 60th anniversary with the theme 'Common things made holy,' featuring over 200 events, including premieres of new performances across various genres. Highlights include classical and contemporary music by artists like Tonje Unstad with the Arctic Philharmonic, Herman Rundberg, Eivør, and Ádjágas, as well as theatrical productions such as Tore Vagn Lid's 'Triggersystemet.' Outdoor performances like 'Le Grande Phrase' and the dance popup JAM will also animate the city. The festival administers arts funding, hosts the Arctic Arts Summit, and includes NUK (The Northern Norwegian Youth Festival).

fields.name: Aré Performing Arts Festival
fields.description: The ARé Performing Arts Festival (ARé FEST) is an 11-day event exploring the intersections of performing and visual arts under the theme 'REALITY SHOW'. It features experimental performances, visual installations, discussions, and workshops by international and local artists, fostering creative exchange and highlighting Armenia's contemporary art scene. Organized by the ARé Cultural Foundation.

fields.name: Athens Epidaurus Festival
fields.description: The Athens Epidaurus Festival celebrates its 70th anniversary in 2025, running from May 28 to August 30. Under the artistic direction of Katerina Evangelatos, the festival features over 100 productions by acclaimed Greek and international artists. Highlights include performances at the Ancient Theatre of Epidaurus, the Odeon of Herodes Atticus, and Peiraios 260, with a focus on ancient Greek drama reinterpreted for contemporary audiences. The program includes theatre, music, and exhibitions, alongside children's workshops. The festival emphasizes a blend of tradition and contemporary artistic creation, with notable productions such as *Antigone*, *Electra*, *Oresteia*, and a world premiere of *Europa's Pledge* starring Juliette Binoche.

fields.name: Gauklerfest
fields.description: The Gauklerfest Attendorn is a vibrant street festival featuring international street artists, musicians, acrobatics, and interactive activities for families. The event includes comedy, music, and a dedicated space for alternative music, along with the 'KleinKunstPreis' ceremony recognizing outstanding performances in the independent arts scene.

fields.name: Tnt Festival Terrassa Noves Tendencies
fields.description: The TNT Festival Terrassa Noves Tendències is dedicated to live arts and new formats, fostering contemporary and multidisciplinary artistic creation. The 2025 edition features 19 scenic proposals, including premieres and international performances, across various venues in Terrassa.

fields.name: Hopla!
fields.description: The 'Hopla!' festival is a circus arts event in Brussels, Belgium, focusing on daytime activities in public spaces. It brings a diverse program of fifteen performances to various squares and districts across Brussels, including De Brouckère, Sainte-Catherine squares, the Versailles and Nord districts, and the Sewer Museum. The festival promotes wonder, curiosity, and inclusivity, transforming urban areas into accessible cultural spaces. It features original creations showcasing Belgian talent, with a core theme of inclusivity, including partnerships with the 'I see' foundation to integrate blind and visually impaired individuals. A notable performance, 'Anchored in Air,' blends circus, dance, spoken word, and music, incorporating wheelchairs and audio description.

fields.name: Miramiro Summer Festival
fields.description: The Miramiro Summer Festival in Ghent, Belgium, is part of the larger Gentse Feesten (Ghent Festival) in 2025. It features a strong emphasis on circus arts, including performances like 'Planeet Sierk - Circusplaneet,' 'Splash - Jam Shenanigans (BE),' 'Tembo - Rode Boom (BE),' 'BEYOND - Circumstances (BE),' and 'iRRooTTaa - Grensgeval & Circus Katoen (BE).' The festival also offers workshops and various circus-related events.

fields.name: Namur In May
fields.description: The 'Namur en Mai' festival, dedicated to fairground arts, transforms the city center of Namur, Belgium, into a vibrant spectacle each May. The event features circus acts, music, cabaret, storytelling, and artisan displays, designed to enchant audiences of all ages. Many street performances are free, making it an accessible open-air celebration during the Ascension weekend. The 2026 edition will mark the festival's 30th anniversary, organized by ASBL NEM with a focus on diverse and rich artistic programming.

fields.name: Summer In Antwerp
fields.description: The 'Zomer van Antwerpen' (Summer of Antwerp) is a city-wide festival transforming Antwerp into a vibrant setting for theater, music, circus, and film across various locations. The 2025 edition (31st) features a mix of local and international artists, with circus performances (including 'Circus Zomerbar'), street theater, live music, and summer bars ('zomerbars') like ZOOmerbar, Bar Noord, and SOMMAR. The festival spans from June 19 to August 31, 2025, with ticket sales starting June 7, 2025. Additional circus events like 'Sinjor Circo' (free Sunday performances in September) are also part of the broader summer program.

fields.name: Chassepierre
fields.description: The Chassepierre International Festival of Street Arts is a 51st edition event celebrating the theme 'Our Words and Gestures,' focusing on the interplay between body and text in performances. Held in the picturesque village of Chassepierre (Florenville, Belgium), the festival transforms the village's squares, alleys, and meadows into vibrant stages for over fifty professional European companies. It features a diverse range of street arts, including theatre, circus, dance, humor, music, puppetry, sideshows, and plastic arts. The festival is renowned for its convivial atmosphere, family-friendly shows, and emphasis on creativity and innovation. It also supports artistic residencies and enhances accessibility with audio descriptions and sign language translations for select performances.

fields.name: Festival Up
fields.description: UP Festival is a significant contemporary circus event organized by UP – Circus & Performing Arts, showcasing both Belgian and international emerging circus forms. The festival features performances, open-air events, and introductions to various circus disciplines.

fields.name: Bitef Festival
fields.description: The 59th Belgrade International Theatre Festival (Bitef) is a prestigious theatre festival facing organizational challenges in 2025. Traditionally held in September, the 2025 edition is proposed to be postponed to the last ten days of November. The festival has been impacted by political turmoil, reduced funding, and changes in artistic leadership, leading to uncertainty about its program and future. The event typically features international theatre performances, though the 2025 edition may have limited international offerings due to budget constraints.

fields.name: Feel Festival
fields.description: The Feel Festival transforms the northern shore of Berghaider Lake into a vibrant cultural event, drawing thousands of attendees from across Europe. It features a diverse range of music genres—including indie, rock, hip-hop, electro, and house—across over 20 stages, alongside creative performances, workshops, discussions, and lectures. The festival emphasizes sustainable and queer feminist themes, fostering community and learning. The 2025 lineup includes collectives like ANIMA, ARNDT29 X VAGINASTAN, BAU122, BCCO, BIPOLAR BODIES, and CURA Berlin, with a tradition of featuring both established and emerging artists.

fields.name: Berlin Lacht
fields.description: The 'Berlin lacht!' (Berlin Laughs!) international street theater festival is an annual event that brings together extraordinary artists from various nations to perform street theater, circus acts, clown theater, puppet theater, and fire shows. The festival aims to revive the lively and colorful atmosphere of the 1920s in Berlin. It is held at two main locations: Kulturstation at Potsdamer Platz (August 5–24, 2025) and Kulturbahnhof at Hauptbahnhof (Washingtonplatz) (August 26–September 15, 2025). Admission is free, and artists rely on audience donations via the 'hungry hat' rule. Organized by the non-profit association 'Berlin lacht! e.V.', the festival promotes street art and culture without state funding.

fields.name: Sounddance Festival
fields.description: The Soundance Festival Berlin is an annual international festival showcasing a diverse array of performances exploring the intersection of dance, music, and text. Founded in 2017, it features choreographed, improvised, instrumental, live, and recorded works, fostering dialogue between disciplines and presenting a broad spectrum of artistic styles from improvisation to choreography, utilizing both analog and electronic approaches. The 2025 edition will include four unique performances inviting audiences to engage with dance, music, movement, sound, language, and rhythm.

fields.name: Buskers Bern
fields.description: The Buskers Bern street music festival transforms the alleys of Bern's Lower Old Town into a vibrant open-air stage with around 300 shows by 140 artists from 25 nations. The festival includes music, dance, theater, acrobatics, comedy, street performance, and puppet theater. Artists perform for 'hat money' and the festival attracts around 70,000 visitors annually.

fields.name: TILT Festival
fields.description: The TILT Festival is a six-day celebration of contemporary circus and aerial artistry, returning to Birmingham for its 9th year in 2025. The event features five days of masterclasses led by world-class aerialists and circus professionals, alongside two days of performances, including a 'Friday Night Pro Night' and 'Saturday All-Day Performances' showcasing young people, emerging artists, and professionals. The festival champions creativity, inclusivity, and sustainability, with workshops for all skill levels, networking opportunities, and a commitment to zero waste. It aims to inspire and empower diverse audiences while addressing critical themes like climate change through circus arts.

fields.name: Artlake
fields.description: The Artlake Brandenburg Festival, traditionally held at Bergheider See, is a street arts festival. As of October 2025, no 2025 edition has been announced. The festival's 2022 edition was themed 're:pair,' but the event was cancelled in 2023. Current activity is focused on the separate 'Artlake Berlin Sessions.'.

fields.name: Theatreformen
fields.description: The Theaterformen festival alternates annually between Hannover and Braunschweig, featuring international theatre, dance, and performance works. The 2026 edition in Braunschweig will showcase contemporary productions by artists addressing often-overlooked themes, alongside conversation formats, city walks, open studios, and Silent Discos. The festival is directed by Anna Mülter and emphasizes political and activist themes.

fields.name: Brighton Festival
fields.description: Brighton Festival is an annual multi-arts festival featuring over 120 events across music, theatre, dance, circus, visual arts, film, literature, debate, outdoor performances, and community activities. The 2025 edition, themed 'A New Dawn,' is guest-directed by Grammy-nominated musician and composer Anoushka Shankar. Highlights include world premieres, immersive performances, participatory events, and collaborations with global artists. The festival offers 45 free events and affordable ticketing options, including the 'Pay It Forward' scheme for community organizations.

fields.name: Spettacolo Brunnen
fields.description: The Spettacolo Brunnen International Street Artist Festival is a biennial event held in Brunnen, Switzerland, featuring international street artists and performances.

fields.name: Artistic Dance Festival
fields.description: The Antistatic Festival is an International Festival for Contemporary Dance and Performance in Bulgaria. The 18th edition in 2025 carries the theme 'Signs of Resistance.' The festival features a diverse program of international performances, educational events, and a 'pay what you can' ticketing policy. It includes a residency program as part of the 'Creative Crossroads' initiative under the 'Life Long Burning' (LLB 3) project.

fields.name: Mini Art Festival
fields.description: The Mini Art Festival, titled 'CIRCOLUTION!', is a contemporary circus festival in its 14th edition. It showcases cutting-edge and socially engaged artists, including circus performers, musicians, DJs, theatre artists, dancers, and storytellers. Organized by the Mini Art Foundation, the festival fosters exchange between the European circus scene and Bulgaria as part of the Circostrada Network since 2019.

fields.name: Buskers Chur
fields.description: The Buskers Chur festival transforms Chur's Old Town into a vibrant cultural arena, featuring over 30 international acts from 20 nations presenting approximately 200 shows across two days. Performances include music, acrobatics, theater, comedy, dance, artistry, and magic, suitable for all ages. The event operates on a 'hat money' principle, where artists' wages depend on audience contributions. Entry is free, but visitors are encouraged to purchase a festival wristband to support the event and its artists. The festival also features around two dozen food stalls offering regional and international street food. The 2025 edition marks the tenth anniversary of this international street art festival.
fields.comments: Applied both via form and then email ...

fields.name: Peculiar Families Festival
fields.description: The Peculiar Families Festival is a contemporary circus festival organized by the ROOM 100 collective, featuring 14 performances by artists from Belgium, Finland, France, Norway, and Germany. The 6th edition includes spectacles, a children's rave party, and an industry program. All events are free and held in public spaces across Split, including peripheral neighborhoods, aiming to make cultural programs accessible to diverse audiences. Highlights include performances like 'PLOCK!', 'Structures', 'DAMOCLÈS', 'Lontano & Instante', 'No Magic', and 'Muovipussi', alongside screenings, discussions, and roundtables.

fields.name: Passage Festival
fields.description: The Passage Festival is one of Northern Europe's largest international street theatre festivals, transforming Helsingør and its surrounding areas into a vibrant stage. The festival showcases high-quality international street theatre, contemporary circus, classic street theatre, site-specific performances, dance, choreography, and experimental performing arts. It brings together around 50 European companies and theatres to present their works in various public spaces, including city centers, residential areas, backyards, cloister gardens, and beaches.

fields.name: Waves Festival
fields.description: The Waves Festival is a prominent international performing arts festival in Denmark, transforming streets, squares, meadows, schoolyards, and theatre halls into vibrant stages. The 2025 edition, curated by a new team, will showcase 19 performing arts companies from 13 countries, featuring dance, performance, contemporary circus, comedy, and visual performing arts. Highlights include international and Danish world premieres, such as 'Don Quixote' by Teatro dei Venti (Italy) and 'BLKDOG' by Botis Seva (UK). The festival also hosts 'WAVES XTRA,' a five-day industry program with conferences, networking, and artist talks, alongside the 'SKVULP!' school program for 17 regional schools. Expected to attract over 20,000 attendees, the festival offers both free and ticketed performances, with discounts for groups and individuals under 25.

fields.name: Brueckensensationen
fields.description: The international street art festival 'Brückensensationen' transforms the Old Rhine Bridge, the 'Inseli,' and other locations into open-air stages for artists from across Europe. It features acrobatics, artistry, clownery, theater, music, and street art, attracting people of all ages.

fields.name: Freiart Festival
fields.description: The Frei Art Festival is an international dance and culture festival held in Freiburg, Germany. Established in 2020, it features a diverse program including intensives and workshops led by internationally acclaimed dance artists (e.g., Ultima Vez, Nederlands Dans Theater), performances by international and local artists, the interdisciplinary Out.liers Dance Contest (September 20, 2025), the Choreographer Summit (Frei Art Connect) with six selected short pieces (September 17, 2025), summer residencies showcasing local artists (opening September 15, 2025), and open-air events with dance, circus, and interactive activities. The festival also includes a benefit concert (September 16, 2025) and a festival party (September 19, 2025). Workshops are open to professionals and experienced amateurs, with bookings available from July 15, 2025. The event transforms Freiburg into a vibrant hub for contemporary and urban dance, fostering artistic excellence, collaboration, and cultural exchange.

fields.name: Hut Festival
fields.description: The 8th HUTFESTIVAL, also known as the Hat Festival Germany, will transform the alleyways and squares in Chemnitz city center into a large open-air stage. It features diverse street artists and acts from 20 nations, including live music, clowning, artistry, juggling, fire shows, imaginative walk acts, art, and street food. The festival area includes nine stages and numerous activity areas, with a 'Market of Beautiful Things' offering selected products and unique items.

fields.name: Impulse Festival
fields.description: The Impulse Festival is a significant platform for independent theatre in the German-speaking world, organized by the NRW KULTURsekretariat in collaboration with the cities of Düsseldorf, Cologne, and Mülheim an der Ruhr. The 2025 edition, under new artistic director Franziska Werner, features a dense program including selected productions from the Showcase, a focus on 'Post-West' (facilitating exchange between East and West German independent scenes), and 'Impulse meets…' (discursive formats, workshops, and parties). The festival spans three long weekends across the three cities, concluding in Düsseldorf with performances and accompanying events at FFT.
fields.town: Mülheim An Der Ruhr, Cologne, Düsseldorf

fields.name: Outnow Festival
fields.description: The OUTNOW! International Performing Arts Festival in Bremen, a biennial event in collaboration with Schwankhalle and Theater Bremen, showcases emerging artists, groups, and collectives from across Europe who are at the beginning of their professional careers.

fields.name: ParkSommer Festival Chemnitz
fields.description: The Parksommer Culture Festival in Chemnitz, Germany, features a diverse open-air program with Indie, Jazz, Swing, Folk, Classical music, Poetry Slams, and Singer-Songwriter performances. The festival includes a special program for children, daily yoga sessions, and evening concerts. All events are free of charge and utilize a 360-degree stage.

fields.name: Palaisommer Dresden
fields.description: The Palais Sommer Dresden 2025 is a 79-day festival offering approximately 200 admission-free events across two main locations: Neumarkt (June 14–July 20) and Alaunpark (July 21–August 24). The program includes themed evenings (Palais.Horizonte) celebrating different nations through music, dance, and culinary experiences; dance nights (Palais.Tanz) with Salsa, Tango, Swing, and Waltz; classical piano concerts (Klaviernacht); panel discussions (Palais.Gespräch); literary events (Palais.Literatur); art exhibitions (FAMA Skulptura); daily morning yoga sessions; and special themed days for seniors, families, and youth. The festival also features a secondhand book market, tombola, photo booth, and a new gastronomic concept with an online reservation system for tables. Committed to free access to art, culture, and education, the festival promotes sustainability, inclusion, and community welfare, financed through donations and community support.

fields.name: Rostfest
fields.description: The Rostfest is an innovative cultural festival in Eisenerz, Austria, thematically linked to the town's iron ore mining heritage ('Rost' meaning 'rust'). For 2025, smaller events like 'Rostfest-Kunsttage,' a 'Kaffeekränzchen,' and a 'Dämmerschoppen' with music acts (e.g., 'Rock im Fuchsbau') are planned as part of a transitional phase, with the full festival expected to return in 2026. The festival uses the theme of 'rust' to reflect on societal changes and urban vacancy, blending art, music, and local history.

fields.name: Circuliacija Festival
fields.description: The International Contemporary Circus Festival 'Cirkuliacija' 2025, themed 'Comebacks (and Discoveries),' features artists returning with new works alongside emerging talents. The festival introduces innovative forms of contemporary circus, fostering connections between audiences and artists. The 2025 lineup includes troupes from Canada, Belgium, Sweden, the Czech Republic, Latvia, and Lithuania, performing across multiple cities in Lithuania.
fields.town: Vilnius, Kaunas, Alytus, Anykščiai, Palanga, Prienai, Tauragė, Naujoji Vilnia

fields.name: Circus Ruska Festival
fields.description: The Circus Ruska Festival, in collaboration with the contemporary circus group Arctic Ensemble, features three full-length contemporary circus performances, an Artistic Seminar for professionals, and a grand finale event called Arctic Ruska Evening. The festival is held in Arctic Ensemble's circus tent at Pyynikintori in Tampere, Finland. All performances are open to all ages, with a separate licensed seating area available for each show. Key performances include Arctic Ensemble's 'ENSEMBLED' (combining acrobatics, music, and community), Armour Circus Works' 'Stygian' (juggling and ambient music), and Cirque Salmenaho's 'Hillasium' (modern and musical contemporary circus). The festival concludes with a cabaret-style show featuring aerial acrobatics, juggling, and human pyramids. A festival pass grants access to all events.

fields.name: Helsinki Festival
fields.description: The Helsinki Festival is the largest multi-arts festival in the Nordic countries, offering a diverse program of classical and contemporary music, theater, dance, circus, visual arts, and urban events. Highlights include the Night of the Arts with free street performances, concerts at Musiikkitalo and the Finnish National Opera, contemporary circus, theater productions, and family-friendly activities. The festival spans 18 days and is organized by the Helsinki Events Foundation under the City of Helsinki.

fields.name: Jojo – Oulu Dance Centre
fields.description: The OuDance Festival is an annual five-day event in Oulu, Finland, featuring diverse dance performances by international and domestic artists. The 2025 edition includes works such as 'ICI JE LÈQUE CE QUI NE M'APPARTIENT PAS' by Habib Ben Tanfous (BE), 'ALIVENESS' by Tanssiteatteri MD, 'GO!' by Jennifer Gohier (LU), 'IT CONTAINS HARD PARTS' by Beatrix Trisha Simkó (HU), and 'OUDANCE – NEW GENERATION'. The festival also offers workshops for professionals, open dance sessions for all ages, and a JoJo Dance Studio Sampler with creative contemporary dance for all levels.

fields.name: Salo Circus Festival
fields.description: The Salo Circus Festival is an international performing arts festival held annually at the break of July and August. It transforms the city of Salo with a diverse program featuring circus, street arts, theater, puppetry, dance, and live music. The festival offers performances for families, enthusiasts, and professionals, with key locations including Salon Teatteri, Kulttuuritalo Kiva, Salon Tori, and Bar Bizarre. Early bird festival passes are available at a discount before the full program and individual show tickets are launched.

fields.name: Silence Festival
fields.description: The Silence Festival Finland is a multidisciplinary event celebrating its 15th anniversary in 2025, featuring contemporary circus, classical music, and spatial design performances. Held in the village of Kaukonen, Kittilä (Lapland), the festival spans over 20 events across unique venues like barns, forests, and improvised stages, with the Ounasjoki river playing a central role in its ambiance. Highlights include world premieres, anniversary pieces directed by Artistic Director Karita Tikka, and a special day at the Särestöniemi Museum honoring the 100th anniversary of Reidar Särestöniemi. The festival blends music, theatre, circus, and visual art into immersive experiences.

fields.name: Tampere Theatre Festival
fields.description: The Tampere Theatre Festival is the oldest and largest professional theatre festival in the Nordic countries, annually transforming Tampere into Finland's theatre capital at the beginning of August. The festival includes a Main Programme, T Lab (concerts, club nights, poetry, and small-scale theatre performances), OFF Tampere, and The Great Nocturnal Happening, alongside seminars, meetings, and discussions. It showcases leading Finnish productions and attracts international guests, with over 55,000 visitors across all programs and events in 2025.

fields.name: Espace Athic / Festival Pisteurs D'Etoiles
fields.description: The 28th edition of the 'Pisteurs d'étoiles' festival is a contemporary circus arts event organized by the association '13e sens – scène & ciné'. The festival features a variety of circus performances, workshops, and installations, many of which are free and take place in various locations throughout Obernai, including a big top tent on the Parking des Remparts. The 2025 program includes performances such as 'HOURVARI' by Compagnie Rasposo and 'Le Chapelier Fou' by Cie Les Escargots Ailés, with a focus on showcasing regional talent in contemporary circus creation.

fields.name: Festival D'Alba La Roraine
fields.description: The Festival d'Alba-la-Romaine is an international circus arts festival that transforms the medieval village and its ancient Roman site into a vibrant hub for live performances. The 16th edition (2025) features renowned international artists, clowns, orchestras, and a diverse program of paid and free shows, concerts, workshops, and archaeological visits. The festival emphasizes accessibility, with adapted spaces, dedicated reception, and free shuttle services. It also includes pre-festival events ('Les Préalables') from June 3–8, 2025, offering free contemporary circus performances across 14 villages in the Ardèche Rhône Coiron territory.

fields.name: Festival des 7 Collines
fields.description: The Festival des 7 Collines is a multidisciplinary international festival held in Saint-Étienne, France, focusing on contemporary circus, dance, performance, and puppetry. Established in 1995, it features innovative creators and projects, offering a modern, inclusive, and accessible cultural experience. The 2025 edition includes performances by Cirque Aïtal, Groupe Acrobatique de Tanger, Mathurin Bolze, and other international artists, alongside residencies, mediation activities, and educational initiatives like 'Cirqu'au Collège' and 'Jeunesse au Cirque'. The festival collaborates with major cultural institutions in Saint-Étienne and extends to neighboring towns, aiming to animate the city and its metropolitan area while fostering rich and unusual artistic encounters.

fields.name: Animaijuin - Petit Quevilly
fields.description: Animaijuin is an annual multidisciplinary festival in Petit-Quevilly, France, featuring street arts, theater, and music. The 2025 edition takes place on Saturday, May 24, from 3:30 PM to midnight at Parc des Chartreux. The event offers family-friendly afternoon activities (musical rhymes, street theater, carousels, wooden games) and evening performances, including soul concerts, a DJ set by Yann Dulché, a rock tribute by Oasis cover band 'Whatever,' and a fireworks finale. Highlights include two performances of 'Kréatures' by Le Chat Fou and a choir performance by local students. Organized by the cultural services of Mairie de Petit-Quevilly, the festival fosters community engagement through diverse entertainment for all ages.

fields.name: chalons dans la rue
fields.comments: Candidatures: From December 1st 2022 to January 10th 2023 incl

fields.name: International Mime Art Festival
fields.description: The 25th International Mime Art Festival in Warsaw is a jubilee edition featuring nine performances (including three premieres), two exhibitions, and masterclass workshops. The festival showcases contemporary mime theater from across Europe, with artists from France, Germany, Russia, USA, Switzerland, Czech Republic, Spain, and Poland. Highlights include performances like 'Verne - Voyages Contemporains,' 'Un Poyo Rojo,' and 'Migrants,' alongside workshops led by renowned artists such as Sara Mangano, Pierre-Yves Massip, and Radim Vizvary. Organized by the Warsaw Mime Centre Foundation and Ursynów Culture Center 'Alternatywy.'.

fields.name: Mime Festival
fields.description: The Mime Fest is an international festival of mime and non-verbal theatre in the Czech Republic. The 14th annual festival, held in Polička at Tylův dům Polička, serves as a significant platform for artists, fostering cooperation with international festivals, schools, and major institutions in the field of mime art. Its program focuses on professional artists and a wide array of mime theatre forms, with a strong emphasis on supporting young talent and educational activities such as workshops and seminars led by experts. These educational programs are designed for both art school students and the general public. The 2025 program features performances and workshops, including 'RED BASTARD (USA)' and 'HOUSE OF MASK & MIME (TH)', as well as a presentation by the National Theatre - Laterna Magika (CZ) titled 'Pluto'. The festival has held the prestigious EFFE label for the best European festivals since 2015.

fields.name: Gbg Mime Fest
fields.description: The GBG Mime Fest is an international mime festival dedicated to showcasing high-quality live performances within the expression of mime. The festival features workshops, masterclasses, and academic discussions in theatre and mime, aiming to expand the understanding of mime in the 21st century. It includes both established and emerging national and international artists, with performances held at venues such as Göteborgs Stadsteater, Folkteatern, and Atalante.
fields.comments: asnwered said to apply on open call “later on”

fields.name: Mimages
fields.description: Mimages is an arts festival dedicated to mime and gestural arts, held in Ardèche, France. Organized by the Communauté de Communes Rhône Crussol, the festival features free performances for schools and two evenings of itinerant performances for the general public. The 19th edition aims to bring together residents, artists, and enthusiasts around a cultural and unifying project, with artistic direction by Philippe Phénieux of the Compagnie Zinzoline.

fields.name: le mans fait son cirque
fields.comments: contacted espace pro to receive and send application

fields.name: Sortie de Bain
fields.description: The Sorties de Bain festival is a family-friendly and free street arts event held in Granville, France. The 23rd edition in 2025 will focus on the theme 'Nature et culture' (Nature and Culture) and feature approximately 120 free performances by 39 companies, including theatre, dance, circus, storytelling, music, object theatre, and magic. The festival takes place in various locations across Granville, such as around the port, facing the beach, and in the city center, attracting around 60,000 spectators annually.

fields.name: Belluard Festival
fields.description: The 42nd edition of the Belluard Festival Fribourg (Belluard Bollwerk) is dedicated to the themes of 'earth, resistance, and solidarity' under the title 'The grounds we share.' This contemporary performing arts festival features 34 projects by Swiss and international artists, exploring colonial legacy, neocolonialism, solidarity, gender, and traditional femininity. It includes works by emerging Latin American artists through 'Ventana al Sur,' and emphasizes hybrid forms of theater, dance, performance, music, visual arts, and public space interventions. The festival fosters discussion, reflection, and community exchange, with events primarily held at the historic Belluard fortress.

fields.name: Atoll
fields.description: The ATOLL Festival for contemporary circus is a pioneering 10-day event in Karlsruhe, Germany, showcasing diverse and innovative circus performances from across Europe and beyond. The 2025 edition, marking the festival's 10th anniversary, features premieres, unique acts, and a special four-day guest performance by French circus star Johann Le Guillerm. The program includes approximately 50 performances curated by Bernd Belschner and Stefan Schönfeld, with a dedicated children's and family day offering free interactive activities. The festival concludes with a participatory event blending performance and celebration.

fields.name: Gassensensationen
fields.description: The 'Gassensensationen' (Alley Sensations) international street theater festival offers free admission and features 46 productions presented by national and international artists across the historic old town and on an open-air stage. Performances include comedy, music theater, acrobatics, puppet shows, and grand plaza productions.

fields.name: La Strada
fields.description: La Strada - International Festival of Street Arts transforms the streets of Bremen into a vibrant stage for international and national artists, featuring acrobatics, dance, theater, clowning, juggling, and installations.

fields.name: Tête-À-Tête
fields.description: The 18th edition of the Tête-À-Tête International Street Theater Festival is recognized as Germany's largest international street theater festival. Held biennially, the festival transforms the baroque city of Rastatt into a vibrant stage for contemporary public theater over five days and six nights. It features a diverse program of artistry, dance, and performances by professional artists from across Europe, showcasing both renowned productions and experimental new talents. The cross-genre program is curated to be entertaining and politically relevant, utilizing outdoor spaces such as streets, squares, and parks. The festival includes free public shows alongside special ticketed performances.

fields.name: Zirkart Festival
fields.description: The ZirkArt Festival in Forchheim, Germany, celebrates the blend of circus arts and theater, featuring acrobatics, juggling, clowning, and more. Organized by Junges Theater Forchheim since 2012, the festival takes place at the KulturSommerQuartier and attracts artists from across Europe. The event includes an opening night, 'VORGLÜHEN,' with free admission, and has gained significant popularity in the region.

fields.name: Viathea
fields.description: The ViaThea International Street Theatre Festival in Görlitz will take place from June 27th to 29th, 2025, with a cultural picnic opening on June 26th in the city park. The festival will feature more than 20 ensembles and artists from various countries including Ireland, Israel, Finland, Germany, France, Italy, Colombia, Mongolia, the Netherlands, Poland, Spain, and the Czech Republic. The festival promises over 200 performances across the city, transforming Görlitz into a large open-air stage.

fields.name: Lendwirbel Festival
fields.description: The Lendwirbel festival is an annual event in Graz that transforms the streets and squares of the Lend district into a vibrant hub of urban coexistence. Known for its diverse program, the festival features concerts, art installations, workshops, discussions, and games, with a focus on community building and critical societal topics. Key locations include Mariahilferplatz, Lendplatz, Volksgarten, and Fröbelpark. The festival emphasizes a bottom-up approach and anti-commercial stance, aiming to strengthen community and collaboratively design public spaces. The 2025 program includes activities such as 'message in a bottle' in Ökonomiegasse, a '10K Steps Challenge' in Volksgarten, 'art.mosphere' on Mariahilferstraße Nord, and a Lendwirbel Poetry Slam on Mariahilferplatz. Music, dance, and creative expression are central to the festival, with daily changing music spots for local artists and street musicians.
fields.comments: Have to propose project to verein!

fields.name: Styrian Autumn
fields.description: The 58th edition of the interdisciplinary festival for contemporary art, 'steirischer herbst '25' (Styrian Autumn '25), explores the theme 'Never Again Peace' (Nie wieder Friede). Inspired by Ernst Toller’s satirical play, the festival reflects on war, political radicalization, and societal polarization through performances, discussions, exhibitions, cabaret, and peace talks. Events are held across Graz and the Styria region, including a central exhibition at the former Bauer distillery (BAU) and partner programs like ORF musikprotokoll and the literature festival Out of Joint. The full program and artist list will be published online on August 11, 2025.

fields.name: Theatreland Styria
fields.description: THEATERland STEIERmark 2.25 is a series of theatre festivals across the Styria region, with key events in Graz. The festival includes 'Graz Erzählt_2025 - Austrian International Storytelling Festival' (April 28–May 9, 2025), featuring performances at Schauspielhaus Graz and Tummelplatz Graz. The broader festival encompasses multiple sub-festivals, such as NEWSOFFSTYRIA (premiere festival) and SOMMERTRAUMHAFEN (international puppet theatre festival), spanning May to September 2025 in various Styrian locations.

fields.name: Athens Circus Festival
fields.description: The Athens Carnival 2025, scheduled from February 15 to March 3, will feature circus performers as part of its festivities. A major celebration with circus artists, music, dancing, and more is planned for February 23 at Zappeion. Historically, the Athens Circus Festival has been a recurring event, with mentions of its 4th and 6th editions in previous years.

fields.name: Noorderzon Festival
fields.description: The Noorderzon Festival of Performing Arts & Society is a 10-day international contemporary performing arts festival held in Groningen, Netherlands. The 35th edition (2025) features a diverse program including theatre, dance, music, literature, film, and interdisciplinary arts, with performances by local and international artists. Many events are accessible to non-Dutch speakers, with language-free shows or English subtitles. The festival emphasizes sustainability, with 80% green grid power and eco-friendly generators. Highlights include free open-air concerts (Apollo stage) and ticketed performances in the Spiegeltent and Lutheran Church, alongside groundbreaking theatre and dance from Catalonia, Lebanon, and China, incorporating AI-driven choreography and urban dance experiences.

fields.name: Larupina
fields.description: The 9th Lurupina International Circus Festival Hamburg is a three-day event for contemporary circus arts, featuring international and regional productions. Highlights include the Lurupina Gala on Friday evening, free admission to the main tent on Saturday and Sunday, and performances by youth circuses and regional initiatives. The festival also offers open-air stages, interactive activities, food stalls, and the Lurupina Bar. Organized by Lichtwark-Forum e.V., it is a significant circus festival in Northern Germany.

fields.name: 5-3-1 festival
fields.comments: Festival for people, objects and circus

fields.name: Tagträumer
fields.description: The domain is associated with the Austrian band 'Tagträumer' (Daydreamers), which performs music events. No festival named 'Daydreamer Festival Horn Horn 2025' was found in Horn, Austria, or linked to this URL. The band's 2026 tour dates are listed for Wiesen and Graz, Austria.

fields.name: Festival Des Arts De La Rue A Huy
fields.description: The Huy Street Arts Festival, formerly known as 'Les Unes Fois d'un Soir,' is a free event featuring over 30 Belgian and international companies with around 90 performances. The program includes fixed and roaming performances, blending intrigue, boldness, madness, poetry, and impertinence. It marks the opening of the street arts festival season in Belgium and includes a professional morning dedicated to performing arts programmers.

fields.name: Theaterfestival Isny
fields.description: The Theaterfestival Isny is a nine-day cultural festival organized by volunteers, featuring diverse performances in a circus tent at Burkwanger Baggersee. The 2025 edition includes acts like David Bowie Theater, Balkan Paradise Orchestra, Familie FLÖZ, and Antilopen Gang, alongside a workshop program with ~60 offerings in singing, instruments, dance, art, and acrobatics. Camping is available on-site, with some dates fully booked.

fields.name: Festival Mirabilia
fields.description: The Mirabilia International Circus & Performing Arts Festival is a five-day immersive experience in live performance, transforming courtyards, gardens, squares, museums, big tops, and woods into stages. The 2025 edition, titled 'People Have the Power,' features over 45 companies from around the world, showcasing contemporary circus, dance, urban theater, and performing arts. The festival emphasizes new languages, emerging artists, and a strong connection with the territory, nature, and community. It includes 3 absolute premieres, 11 national premieres, and 16 regional debuts, with a special focus on Chile. The 'Mirabilia On the Road' program extends the festival to other municipalities in Piedmont from July 6 to September 28, 2025.

fields.name: La Luna Nel Pozzo
fields.description: The 30th edition of the La Luna nel Pozzo International Street Theatre Festival in Caorle, Italy, featuring over 70 shows by 22 international companies. The festival transforms the seaside village into a widespread stage with performances including acrobatics, clowning, puppet theatre, contemporary circus, and juggling. Events are held in the streets and squares of Caorle, with additional locations in Ca' Corniani and Corso Genova in Porto Santa Margherita. The festival is free to attend.

fields.name: Lonato In Festival
fields.description: Lonato in Festival is a contemporary circus festival held at the Rocca di Lonato del Garda in Brescia, Italy. The 2025 edition, its thirteenth, features a new format with a single prestigious contemporary circus company, MagdaClan Circo, presenting their show 'ECCEZIONE n. 24.' The performance combines acrobatics, live music, and poetic moments, offering a unique experience each evening. Performances begin at 9:30 PM, with tickets priced at €10 for adults and €5 for children aged 4 to 14.

fields.name: Lunathica
fields.description: The Lunathica International Street Theatre Festival is a 5-day event featuring over 150 free performances by 80 artists from 28 international companies. The festival spans seven municipalities in the Turin area, showcasing a diverse range of acts including circus, music, comedy, acrobatics, contemporary circus, puppet theatre, modern dance, and juggling. Performances take place in unusual locations such as town squares, courtyards, natural settings, and shop windows. The festival includes 9 national premieres and 10 regional premieres, with a central hub at the 'Circus Village' in Parco Due Laghetti, Villanova Canavese, offering food, games, and sustainable activities.
fields.town: Turin Area (Cirié, Mathi, Nole, Fiano, Lanzo Torinese, Leinì, Villanova Canavese)

fields.name: Mercanti A Certaldo
fields.description: Mercantia, the International Festival of the Fourth Theater, transforms the medieval village of Certaldo into an open-air stage under the theme 'The land of a dream.' The 37th edition features over 40 theater companies and 100+ artists performing dance, music, juggling, figure theater, cabaret, marching bands, and circus acts. The festival includes a market with culinary delights and antique stalls. Access to Certaldo Bassa is free, while Certaldo Alta requires a ticket.

fields.name: Tutti Matti Per Colorno
fields.description: Tutti Matti per Colorno is a vibrant street arts festival held annually in Colorno, Italy. The event features performances, workshops, and interactive installations, transforming the town into a lively open-air stage for artists and spectators.

fields.name: Composé Festival
fields.description: The Composé Festival Jena 2025, also known as Composé DAYS 2025, is a unique festival in Jena and Central Germany focusing on contemporary circus art and music. The event features a variety of performances, including circus shows, urban dance workshops, and live music concerts. It is held at Circus MoMoLo, Burgauer Weg 9, 07745 Jena, and is organized by MoMoLo e.V. Workshops and some performances require prior registration via email.

fields.name: Klagenfurt Promenade
fields.description: The Klagenfurt Festival 2025 will animate the Old Town with a diverse program including theater, performance, music, dance, video, technology, literature, and pop culture. The festival opens on May 23 with performances by Austrofred and Buntspecht, and features acts such as Bonaparte, Kovacs, Lars Eidinger, Bibiana Beglau, Philipp Hochmair, Soap&Skin, Clara Luzia, and Benjamin von Stuckrad-Barre. Political themes will be addressed with performances by Tiger Lillies and Pussy Riot.

fields.name: Klagenfurt Festival
fields.description: The Klagenfurt Festival 2025 is the 5th edition of this international cultural event, offering a diverse program encompassing theater, performance, music, dance, video, technology, literature, and pop culture. Highlights include performances by Austrofred, Buntspecht, Philipp Hochmair, Clara Luzia, Ben Becker, Lars Eidinger, Pussy Riot, and The Tiger Lillies. The festival transforms Klagenfurt's old town and various other locations into a vibrant hub of cultural activities, featuring music, theater, dance, and literature from renowned artists and collectives.

fields.name: Circus Dance Festival
fields.description: The Circus Dance Festival Cologne celebrates the diversity of contemporary circus, dance, and performance. The festival features over 20 international artists, presenting a variety of formats across tent stages and two 360° open-air stages. Highlights include performances, a European conference on mental health in circus arts, concerts, and film programs. The festival aims to provide a poetic, thought-provoking, political, and spectacular experience, focusing on the language of the body.

fields.name: Chuncheon Mime Festival
fields.description: The Chuncheon International Mime Festival is one of the world's top three mime festivals, celebrating its 37th edition in 2025. Held annually in Chuncheon City, the festival transforms urban and natural spaces into stages for diverse performances, including street theater, physical theater, dance, circus, installation art, and site-specific works. The 2025 theme, 'Body Scape (몸풍경),' explores the interplay between bodies and landscapes, creating a unique artistic experience centered on movement and imagery. The festival features hundreds of local and international mime artists, with key programs such as *Opening Nanjang: Ah! Surajang*, *All-Night Nanjang: Dokkaebi Nanjang*, *Hello? Mime House*, *Everyone's Spring*, *Walking Mime*, and *Dokkaebi Troupe*. Venues include Chuncheon Jungang-ro, Festival Theater MOMZIT, Commons Field Chuncheon, Seoksacheon walking trail, and Legoland Korea Resort.

fields.name: RE RĪGA!
fields.description: RE RĪGA! International Contemporary Circus and Street Art Festival is an annual cultural event in the Baltics since 2013. It showcases contemporary circus and street performances by international artists, held at Rīgas Cirks and Vērmane Garden Park. The festival aims to invigorate Riga's cultural scene and foster the growth of circus arts in Latvia and the Baltic region.

fields.name: Euro-Scene Leipzig
fields.description: The Euro-Scene Leipzig is the 35th International Dance and Theater Festival, featuring 14 productions with 33 individual events, including one world premiere, three German premieres, and five co-productions. Guest performances from countries such as Belgium, Brazil, Germany, France, Canada, Austria, Switzerland, Serbia, Slovenia, South Africa, and Uganda are expected. The festival takes place at various venues across Leipzig, Germany.

fields.name: Cirkl
fields.description: The eighth edition of Cirkl Leuven, a contemporary circus festival held at the Philipssite in Leuven. The festival interweaves urban disciplines such as BMX, breaking, and parkour with contemporary circus arts. It features three days of high-quality performances for all ages, including national and international artists, premieres, accessible shows, and experimental productions. The program spans indoor, outdoor, and unexpected urban spaces. Cirkl 2025 also introduces its first international programmers' program, focusing on Flemish productions, work-in-progress presentations, and professional exchange. Organized by 30CC and CIRKLABO in collaboration with Cirkus in Beweging and the City of Leuven.

fields.name: Olala Lienz
fields.description: The Olala Street Theatre Festival in Lienz, Austria, is an international event transforming the town into a vibrant open-air cultural arena. The 34th edition (2025) features a diverse program of street art, theater, and circus performances, including acrobatics, non-verbal comedy, walk acts, fanfare bands, juggling, interactive productions, and contemporary circus. Approximately 32 groups from 15 countries participate across various locations, including the city center, Iselkai, and the festival ground around Dolomitenhalle. Highlights include the Olala midday parade on Saturday, a new night parade at Iselkai, and performances like 'L'Absolu' by Les Choses de Rien and 'Flying Piano' by the Cardinali Group. The festival combines art, theater, and circus to present contemporary performative staging, with both free outdoor performances and ticketed indoor shows.

fields.name: Linz Pflasterspektakel
fields.description: The Linz Pflasterspektakel is an international street performance festival held in the city center of Linz, Austria. This three-day event showcases a diverse range of street art, including comedy, clowning, juggling, acrobatics, dance, figure and object theatre, magic, high-wire artistry, fire shows, and musical performances. Over 100 companies and solo artists from around the world transform Linz's city center into a vibrant open-air stage. Performances occur hourly at approximately 40 different locations. Artists perform for 'Hutgeld' (hat money) from the audience, and the daily program is decided each morning of the festival days, reflecting the spontaneous nature of street art.

fields.name: ana desetnica - international street art festival
Maribor Theatre Festival
fields.description: The Maribor Theatre Festival, also known as Borštnikovo srečanje, is Slovenia's most important theatre festival. The 60th edition in 2025 will feature a competitive program of 12 Slovenian productions curated by Ajda Rooss, alongside international guest performances from Croatia, Italy, France, and Greece. The festival includes student theatre productions, workshops, discussions, exhibitions, and roundtables. The prestigious Borštnikov Ring award is presented for outstanding acting careers. The event spans multiple venues in Maribor, including the Slovenian National Theatre, and has evolved into an international platform engaging with the European and global theatre scene.

fields.name: Locomotion
fields.description: The Performance Platform Festival (PPF) aims to foster a vibrant choreographic and dance ecosystem in North Macedonia, amplifying the visibility of dance and choreographic practices. The June 2025 edition features an '(Eco)Feminism in Practice(s): Rethinking Art and Curatorial Futures' (un)conference, the first edition of the Macedonian Choreographic Platform (MCP), and performances such as 'BLOT – Body Line of Thought'.

fields.name: Mladi Levi
fields.description: The 28th International Mladi Levi (Young Lions) Festival is an annual contemporary performing arts event organized by the Bunker Institute. It focuses on original approaches to theatre and dance, with a particular emphasis on emerging young creators. The 2025 edition explores themes of temporality and the future through various emotions, featuring performances by young theatre artists such as Filip Mramor, Aljoša Lovrić Krapež, Aline Olmos Steler, Ajda Pirtovšek & Čarna Lampret, Mélissa Guex, Alice, and Davide Sinigaglia. The festival opens with performances like 'Stinky Concert on Four Paws' and 'Is Joy II', followed by an opening party with DJs Minjas and Ajsi. Tickets for individual events are symbolically priced at 1 EUR.

fields.name: Boulevard Theater Festival
fields.description: The Theaterfestival Boulevard 2025 is the 41st edition of this vibrant festival, featuring approximately 125 different theater and dance performances, concerts, and workshops by national and international artists. The festival transforms the city center of 's-Hertogenbosch into a lively stage for eleven days, with events held in various indoor and outdoor locations, including theater halls, tents, and gardens. The central hub is the Parade, located at the foot of the Sint Jan cathedral. The 2025 edition's motto is 'Radical Wonder' ('In gemeenschap van verwondering'), emphasizing curiosity and receptiveness to the world. The festival offers both ticketed and free events, creating a dynamic and inclusive atmosphere.

fields.name: paderborn trade fair
fields.approximate_date: Not a festival but a trade fair

fields.name: Carnaval Sztuk-Mistrzów
fields.description: The Carnaval Sztukmistrzów (Carnival of Masters of Arts) is the largest festival in Poland dedicated to contemporary circus arts, held in Lublin. The 16th edition (2025) features a diverse program under the theme 'RUN AWAY WITH THE CIRCUS,' including street shows, theatrical-circus spectacles, workshops, and fire shows. Highlights include acrobatics, clowning, juggling, illusions, and the concurrent 17th Urban Highline Festival with highliners performing above the city. Paid performances include Gabriela Muñoz's 'Julieta,' My!Laika's 'Winter,' and shows by Baltic Nordic Circus Network artists. Free events include street performances, buskers' competition, circus animation zones, slackline workshops, and the Frikart Circus Town. Most events are free, with select performances requiring tickets via stage24.pl.

fields.name: Circulaje
fields.description: The 12th Eastern European Contemporary Circus Festival 'Cyrkulacje' showcases spectacles from contemporary circus masters, an international Etude Competition, premieres, and accompanying events. Organized by Fundacja Sztukmistrze, the festival features performances like 'Slavic' (Polish premiere) and 'Spektrum,' alongside acts by Compagnie Common Ground, Scenic Circus, and Circus Folie. The event emphasizes new circus forms without animal acts, focusing on skill, artistry, and accessibility, including spaces for people with motor disabilities and Polish Sign Language (PJM) translations for some performances.

fields.name: Malta Festival Poznań
fields.description: The Malta Festival Poznań 2025 is a multidisciplinary festival marking its 35th anniversary, featuring theatre, music, outdoor performances, film, literature, and discussion panels. The 2025 edition emphasizes 'feminine energy' with a focus on women's perspectives and themes. Highlights include performances by international artists like Róisín Murphy, The Teskey Brothers, and Tilda Swinton, as well as world premieres, aerial performances, and free public events across Poznań's venues and public spaces.

fields.name: Ofca Festival
fields.description: The OFCA Festival is an international contemporary circus and art festival transforming Oleśnica into a stage for New Circus. It features over 60 shows, including street performances, theatrical-circus spectacles, workshops, music, and performative acts. The 11th edition (2025) introduces new locations such as Princes of Oleśnica Park and I High School sports field, alongside highlines across the Market Square and Castle, circus tents, fire shows, and a festival club. Artists from 22 countries participate, with highlights including Fabrizio Solinas' 'Little Garden' and the duo Chris-Iris' 'GAP of 42'. The event also includes workshops for children, a food truck zone, and a commemorative mural created with young artists.

fields.name: up to you - international festival of contemporary creation
Ruhr Festival
fields.description: The Ruhrfestspiele Recklinghausen is one of Europe's oldest and largest theatre festivals, established in 1946. The 2025 edition, themed 'Doubt and Cohesion,' runs from May 1 to June 8, featuring over 90 productions and 220 events across genres like drama, dance, literature, new circus, youth theatre, music, and cabaret. Highlights include world and German premieres, performances by international artists, and installations by renowned creators like Judy Chicago. The festival begins with a large public cultural event on May 1st, followed by the official opening on May 3rd with a literary address by Cécile Wajsbrot. Venues include the Ruhrfestspielhaus, Halle König Ludwig 1/2, Theater Marl, and various city-center locations.

fields.name: Kulturpflaster
fields.description: Kulturpflaster (Cultural Pavement) is an international street circus festival in Regensburg, organized by the Mischkultur e.V. association.

fields.name: Sibiu International Theater Festival
fields.description: The Sibiu International Theatre Festival (FITS) is a major cultural event featuring over 842 performances, including theatre, dance, contemporary circus, classical and contemporary music, exhibitions, conferences, and workshops. The 2025 edition, themed 'Thank You,' celebrates the connection between artists and audiences, with participation from over 5,000 artists from 82 countries. Notable attendees include Bill Murray, Kathleen Turner, Wole Soyinka, Kuranosuke Sasaki, Alain Platel, and Alexis Michalik. Many events are free, while ticketed performances range from 30 to 150 lei, with online viewing options available for select events.

fields.name: La Bâtie Festival
fields.description: The La Bâtie-Festival de Genève is a multidisciplinary festival transforming Geneva into an open stage, featuring over 50 events and 100 performances across 40 venues. The 2025 edition celebrates dance, theater, music, and circus, showcasing both emerging talents and established artists. Highlights include performances by Boris Charmatz, Lia Rodrigues, François Chaignaud, Cirque Trottola, Peter Bjorn & John, and local artists like Chien Bleu and Magic & Naked. The festival opens with the dance piece '1000&1 BPM_Odyssée' and closes with Boris Charmatz's '10'000 gestes'. The event also features the 'SIX/SIX' project for emerging artists and a dedicated festival restaurant, La Réplique.

fields.name: BewegGrund
fields.description: The BewegGrund.Das Festival is a dance festival focused on accessibility and inclusion, promoting cultural participation for people with and without disabilities. The 14th edition features national and international dance productions by professional dancers, community dance groups, and workshops with international guests. The festival aligns with the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities, aiming to remove physical, communicative, and institutional barriers in art and culture. Venues include Dampfzentrale Bern and Heitere Fahne in Wabern bei Bern. Collaborators include disframe, Migros-Kulturprozent, and partner festivals Wildwuchs (Basel), Out of the Box (Geneva), and Orme (Lugano).

fields.name: Act Festival Sofia
fields.description: The ACT Festival Sofia is an annual forum for autonomous artists and organizations, promoting new artistic practices and providing an exchange platform for national and international experts in independent theater, dance, and performance. It showcases hundreds of performances, workshops, discussions, and fosters national and international partnerships.

fields.name: Kleinkunst Usedom
fields.description: The International Cabaret Festival Usedom (Internationales Kleinkunstfestival Insel Usedom) is a vibrant street art festival held along the promenade around Heringsdorf pier on Usedom Island. The 24th edition in 2025 will feature a diverse program of street performances, including magic, pantomime, clowns, juggling, acrobatics, street theater, comedy, animation, and fire shows across eight venues. The festival is free to attend, with artists performing for donations. Highlights include the 'Varieté am Meer' gala program in the Chapeau Rouge theater tent, a craft market, food stalls, and a 'participation circus' for younger visitors. International artists such as HEROMACRO (Japan), Mr Paradise (Argentina), Kalayo Fire Collective, Duo Meroni Zamboni, Cyr Siux Circus (Chile), Disco Balance (Brazil/Peru), Mr Banana (Canada), The Gipsy Marionettist, Wab & Étincelles, Circus Simon, and Rumba Company will perform.

fields.name: Artists De Rue Vevey
