fields.description: The International Street Artists Festival in Vevey is an annual event featuring around 20 international troupes performing over 170 shows, including jugglers, mimes, actors, acrobats, fire-breathers, and clowns. The festival takes place in the Old Town of Vevey, with Place Scanavin as its central hub. Entrance is free, and artists accept contributions via 'au chapeau' (hat collection) at the end of their performances. The public can vote for their favorite acts, which are awarded the 'Pavés d'Or, d'Argent et de Bronze' (Gold, Silver, and Bronze Paving Stones). The 2025 edition will feature artists such as Bobby Garden (Germany), Yldor (Catalonia), Anna Ki (Estonia), Domenyk La Terra (Australia), Daigoro (Italy), Andy Spigola (Italy), Fire Ninja (Australia), and Jama Juggling (Uzbekistan), among others. The festival also serves as a platform for local artists and circus schools from French-speaking Switzerland.

fields.name: Street Art Festival
fields.description: The Villach Street Art Festival transforms the old town into a magical world with jugglers, pantomimes, and artists. This free festival features around 100 international artists from ten nations, offering music, pantomime, comedy, and interactive performances. As dusk falls, fire breathers and fire jugglers take the stage. The event relies on audience donations collected after each performance.

fields.name: Werne On The Lippe
fields.description: The Sparkassen-StraßenFESTIVAL Werne is a three-day street festival celebrating its tenth anniversary in 2025. It features 28 artists from four continents and ten countries, presenting 15 diverse acts including juggling, fire performances, marionette theater, live music, comedy, acrobatics, and magic. Highlights include 'Fire Nights' on Friday and Saturday, and a dedicated children's program with interactive activities and Argentine marionette theater. The event takes place on Werne's historic cobblestone streets and is supported by the city of Werne and Werne Marketing.

fields.name: On The Edge
fields.description: Vienna's first festival for experimental circus arts, ON THE EDGE 2025 features innovative acrobatics, artistic experiments, and politically charged performances across multiple venues. The program includes a diverse lineup of shows, a symposium titled 'I WANNA CIRCUS WITH YOU!' for networking and discussions on contemporary circus trends, and a pitching session for Austrian circus productions. Venues include Theater am Werk (Kabelwerk and Petersplatz) and Dschungel Wien.

fields.name: Wiener Neustadt Street Festival
fields.description: The Strassen.Kunst.Festival (Street Art Festival) transforms the city center of Wiener Neustadt into a vibrant stage for regional and international street artists. The event features a diverse program including music, artistry, acrobatics, magic, pantomime, diabolo acts, juggling, street comedy, and spectacular fire shows in the evenings. The festival spans eight performance locations in the main square and pedestrian zones, offering activities for all ages. Entry is free, with artists relying on audience donations ('Hutgeld').

fields.name: Zurich Theatre Spectacle
fields.description: The Zurich Theatre Spectacle is an international festival for contemporary performing arts, featuring theatre, dance, contemporary circus, concerts, talks, street art, open-air performances, installations, and family-friendly activities. Held at Landiwiese park by Lake Zurich, the festival offers a diverse program with artists from around the world, alongside restaurants, bars, and artistic interventions.

fields.name: Front@
fields.description: The International Dance Festival Front@, celebrating its 20th anniversary, will take place in Murska Sobota, Slovenia. The event features top dance works, workshops, and discussions with guest artists from around the world, including Brazil, South Korea, the Netherlands, Belgium, Italy, France, Croatia, and Slovenia. Venues include Gledališče Park and the castle surroundings. The program includes performances by renowned international and Slovenian artists, such as French choreographer Leïla Ka, Dutch group Panama Pictures, South Korean dancer Jeong Bin Seo, Croatian dancer Matea Bilosnić, Italian duo Panzetti / Ticconi, Slovenian duo Fabla, and Czech-Slovenian duo Kristýna Peldova and Enya Belak. DJ Obito will provide a relaxed atmosphere for socializing.

fields.name: fira de circ al carrer la bisbal d'emporda
Duckstein Festival
fields.description: A ten-day event in HafenCity, Hamburg, offering a mix of culinary delights, live music, design, and theater. Held annually at Magdeburger Hafen.
fields.comments: Doesn't seem to be a festival

fields.name: Street Artist Festival In Scharbeutz
fields.description: The Street Art Festival (Straßenkunst Festival) in Scharbeutz, Germany, transforms the resort into a lively festival ground with international artists from countries such as Chile, Finland, South Africa, Israel, Venezuela, Argentina, Germany, and Italy. The event features a diverse program including acrobatics, juggling, artistry, clownery, magic, and street performances across four stages in the Kurpark, promenade, and beach. Performances run daily from afternoon until late night. Admission is free, with artists compensated through voluntary audience donations. The festival also includes an artist school for children aged 5 and up, offering workshops in artistry, juggling, and circus techniques. Spectators can vote for their favorite artist for the 'Goldener Hut' (Golden Hat) award, with the top three invited back for the following year's festival. The event also features lounges, craft stalls, and culinary delights. Organized by the Tourismus-Agentur Lübecker Bucht.

fields.name: Kunsten Op Straat
fields.description: The Heimland Festival transforms the town of Diepenheim into a vibrant stage for theatre, art, philosophy, music, and dance. The 2025 edition's theme is 'Neighborliness in all its colors' ('Noaberschap in al z'n kleuren'). Access on Friday, June 13th is free for all visitors. For Saturday and Sunday, a festival wristband is required, though visitors aged 21 and younger can obtain a free wristband. Some performances may require separate tickets.

fields.name: Pinneberg Cabaret Festival
fields.description: The 'Pinneberg Festival - 150 Jahre Wir-Gefühl' (150 Years of We-Feeling) is a large anniversary celebration transforming the city center into a party zone. The event features live music, theater, dance, singing, and potentially cabaret-style performances as part of its diverse program, marking the 150th anniversary of Pinneberg.

fields.name: Koblenz Gauklerfestung
fields.description: The Gauklerfestung (Juggler Fortress) in Koblenz, Germany, features over 150 international street artists, comedians, acrobats, cabaret artists, and walk acts. The festival includes stage performances, walk acts, and street theater at various locations and in the trenches of the Ehrenbreitstein Fortress.

fields.name: Friedrichshafen Kulturufer
fields.description: A ten-day cultural event transforming the Friedrichshafen lakeside promenade into a vibrant stage, offering a diverse program for all ages. Features concerts, theater, dance, comedy, open-air cinema, street art, workshops, and a craft market.

fields.name: Busker'S Bus
fields.description: The Busker Tour 2025 is a traveling festival of performing and circus arts across multiple cities in Poland. It features over 30 groups of international street artists showcasing circus acts, music, pantomime, clowning, juggling, unicycling, fire shows, acrobatics, and more. Performances are free, operating on a street theatre model where artists appreciate audience tips. The festival is curated by artistic director Małgorzata Węglarz and includes artists from countries such as Australia, Taiwan, Japan, Hong Kong, Chile, Austria, Ireland, the USA, Argentina, Germany, the UK, Italy, and Romania. In Wrocław, shows occur in the Market Square (2 PM–10 PM) and later move to the festival club Dziady i Koktajle.
fields.town: Żary, Krotoszyn, Zielona Góra, Zduńska Wola, Wrocław
fields.comments: apply beginning of january closes very quickly!!

fields.name: Cirkul'Art Festival
fields.description: Cirkul'art is an international festival of New Circus, the only event of its kind in Slovakia. It features creative and genre-crossing artistic productions focused on contemporary theatre and modern circus, characterized by narrative storytelling through circus arts to convey metaphors, stories, themes, and theatrical imagery. The 2025 edition includes performances by artists from Peru, Argentina, Spain, France, Italy, the UK, Germany, Ireland, Poland, and Moldova, with events held primarily in the castle park of Malacky and select locations in Bratislava (e.g., Eurovea shopping center).

fields.name: Circulum Festival
fields.description: The Cirkulum Festival is an international festival of new circus and street theater, featuring a rich program of new circus, pantomime, street, movement, and improvisational theater. The ninth edition in 2025 will span eight days, a first in its history, and will include performances by world-renowned artists such as Alexander Koblikov, Les Rois Vagabonds, and Le Mur De La Mort. The festival also introduces a children's theater tent, with shows like 'Poppy and Adélka' and 'Orange Ship,' coinciding with International Children's Day. Main events are held at Silesian Ostrava Castle, with additional 'Cirkulum on the Road' performances in other locations like Krnov.

fields.name: festival les années joué, joué les tours
Cest Is Best
fields.description: The 'Cest is d'Best' International Street Festival is the oldest and largest street festival in Croatia, held continuously since 1997. It transforms Zagreb's city center into a large open-air stage, offering a diverse program of music, drama, art, sports, and dance. The festival features performances from participants across Croatia and approximately 15 other countries, with main stages at Zrinjevac, Ban Jelačić Square, Flower Square, and additional performances in streets such as Bogovićeva, Gajeva, Praška, and Tkalčićeva. The EU Square also serves as a festival location, featuring street performers, musicians, and a 'd Best Street Food corner.' The program is designed for all generations and includes concerts, artistic and entertainment programs, dance performances, circus acts, visual arts, and even a traditional street-sweeper tricycle race.

fields.name: Spancirfest
fields.description: Špancirfest, Croatia's celebrated street festival, transforms the historic city center of Varaždin into a vibrant open-air stage for ten days. The 27th edition (2025) features a diverse program blending music, art, and entertainment, including street performances by acrobats, stilt walkers, drummers, and musicians, alongside concerts, theater acts, workshops, and exhibitions. Highlights include performances by Disco Opera, S.A.R.S., Gipsy Kings by Diego Baliardo, YB (Yoon Do Hyun Band), and Dino Jelusić across multiple stages. The festival also offers gastronomic experiences such as the Carlsberg Food & Party zone, Špancir Burger Festival, 'Wine Town' with 350+ wine labels, and new zones like 'Tastes of the World' and a 'Retro zone' dedicated to the 70s–90s. Emphasizing sustainability, Špancirfest uses recycled materials for accreditations and merchandise, aiming to create an unforgettable experience for all ages.

fields.name: Ulica Festival Krakow
fields.description: The Ulica Festival, Kraków's renowned International Street Theatre Festival, celebrates its 38th edition in 2025 with the theme 'Dance… Dance… Dance…'. This free event transforms key squares and open spaces across Kraków into open-air stages, featuring over 180 artists from countries like Poland, South Korea, France, Spain, and the Netherlands. The program includes contemporary and experimental dance, monumental visual theatre, fire shows, aerial performances, puppet theatre, and non-verbal clowning and mime. Notable acts include Voalá (Spain) with an aerial spectacle, ICONTAT (South Korea) combining masked dance with EDM and hip-hop, and Teatr KTO (Poland) presenting local legends. The festival takes place in two stages: July 4–6 and July 13–15, 2025.

fields.name: Festiwal Dwoch Jezeior
fields.description: The 9th edition of the 'Festiwal Dwóch Jezior' (Festival of Two Lakes) is a three-day celebration of music, sports, and leisure activities in Wałcz, Poland. The event features concerts by artists such as Sarsa, IGO, Rat Kru, and Brathanki, alongside a traditional Shanty Evening with bands like Klang, North Cape, and Majtki Bosmana. The festival also includes sailing regattas, Dragon Boat competitions, tournaments, and the 14th Wałecki Bieg Filmowy (Film Run). Attractions span a children's zone, inflatable castle festival, amusement park, artistic workshops, foam party, Star Parade, DJ workshops, Freestyle Battle, city game, circus performance, and historical/post-apocalyptic villages. Admission is free.

fields.name: Muja Buskers Festival
fields.description: The Muja Buskers Festival is a contemporary circus and street theatre event featuring performances such as music, clowning, tightrope balancing, juggling, aerial rope, and puppet shows. The festival also includes circus workshops for children and young people, aiming to create a magical journey through contemporary circus expressions and foster interactions between artists and the public. Now in its ninth edition, the event takes place in Muggia, Italy.

fields.name: Off The Radar Festival (Music & Art)
fields.description: Off the Radar Festival is a decentralized city festival in Hamburg, focusing on a diverse range of music from avant-garde to punk, alongside performance and art in public and unconventional spaces. Venues include 3001 KINO, B-MOVIE, BEAT BOUTIQUE, DEZENTRALE, EGALBAR, FREIHEIT & ROOSEN, GARTENKUNSTNETZ, GÄNGEVIERTEL, GOLDEN PUDEL, HAFENKLANG, HOCHWASSERBASSIN, KACHELRAUM, MARKTHALLE, MS STUBNITZ, OBST & GEMÜSE, and SLOT.

fields.name: Wilde Möhre
fields.description: The Wilde Möhre Festival is an annual event near Cottbus, Germany, known for its focus on electronic music (electro, house, techno) alongside diverse genres like pop, hip hop, indie, and punk. The festival emphasizes sustainability, community, and inclusivity, offering workshops on topics such as sustainability, personal development, creative writing, and yoga. It features eco-friendly practices, vegetarian/vegan food options, and a non-commercial atmosphere without VIP areas. Approximately 5,000–7,000 attendees are expected.

fields.name: Bamberg
fields.description: Bamberg zaubert is an international street and variety festival in Bamberg, Germany, marking its 25th anniversary in 2025. The historic old town transforms into a magical stage with over 100 artists from around the world. The festival features magic, acrobatics, juggling, fire and light shows, and live music across more than 10 open-air venues. Highlights include the 'Magical Nights' on Maximiliansplatz and spectacular fire shows in the city center. As one of Europe's largest free magic and street festivals, it expects around 250,000 visitors in 2025. Organized by Stadtmarketing Bamberg e.V., entry is free for all visitors.

fields.name: Theater Op De Mark
fields.description: The 14th edition of Theater op de Markt, a circus festival held at Provinciaal Domein Dommelhof in Pelt, Belgium. The festival features a diverse program of performances, including works by Akoreacro, Arno Ferrera & Gilles Polet, Begüm Erciyas, Boris Gibé, and many others. It also includes an expo titled 'Carrousel der verwondering' celebrating 30 years of D'irque&Fien, thematic conversations on circus topics, and a pitching session for artists and programmers in collaboration with Circuscentrum.

fields.name: Kultursommer Wien
fields.description: The Kultursommer Wien (Vienna Summer of Culture) offers free open-air cultural events across Vienna, featuring over 500 acts and 2,000 artists across various genres, including music, cabaret, literature, dance, performance, contemporary circus, and theater. The festival emphasizes accessibility and community engagement with workshops, open art stations, and family-friendly programs.

fields.name: Villach Festival
fields.description: The Villach Street Art Festival transforms the old town into a vibrant stage for jugglers, mimes, and artists. Visitors can expect a colorful spectacle featuring clowns, jugglers, and performers offering music, pantomime, and comedy, providing entertainment for all ages. Approximately 100 international artists from ten different nations are celebrated by an enthusiastic audience. The festival is free to attend, and artists rely on donations from visitors, collected in a hat after each performance. As dusk falls, fire-eaters and fire jugglers take center stage.

fields.name: Behind The Door
fields.description: The 17th edition of the 'Za dveřmi' (Behind the Door) Czech street theatre festival, featuring a diverse program of street theatre, new circus, dance, and music. Highlights include international acts like Lucamoros (France), Federico Menini (Argentina), and Imre Bernath (Hungary), as well as Czech ensembles such as Bratři v tricku and V.O.S.A. Theatre. The festival includes a dedicated Puppet Day on July 12 with a giant puppet procession and a Family Day on July 13 with workshops and fairy tales. All events are free to attend with voluntary admission.

fields.name: Divadlo Festival
fields.description: The International Theatre Festival (Mezinárodní festival Divadlo) in Plzeň, Czech Republic, is a prestigious event showcasing exceptional international productions alongside the best Czech theatrical works from the past season. The 33rd edition in 2025 will feature a diverse program including drama, musical theatre, dance, puppet theatre, and street theatre. Highlights include performances such as 'Vzdálenost' by Tiago Rodrigues, 'Svatá Johanka z jatek' by Bertolt Brecht (performed by the Berliner Ensemble), 'Parallax' by Kata Wéber, and 'Tatarka' by Dominik Tatarka and Dávid Paška. The festival also includes a section called 'Johan uvádí…' featuring work-in-progress projects and dance/performative acts at the Moving Station. Accompanying events include debates, exhibitions, and outdoor activities, many of which are free.

fields.name: Mimefest
fields.description: The 14th International Mime Festival (MIME FEST) is a prestigious event dedicated to contemporary mime and non-verbal theatre. The festival showcases diverse forms of mime, from traditional to experimental, and supports emerging artists through workshops and seminars. The 2025 program, subtitled 'Let it mime,' features renowned international and local artists, including performances by American clown Red Bastard, Thai ensemble House of Mask & Mime, German mime Silent Rocco, and the Czech National Theatre's Laterna Magika. The festival also includes professional workshops and culminates in a 'Mime Evening' gala featuring mime, clowning, grotesque, and new circus acts.

fields.name: Komediani Vulicic
fields.description: The 'Komedianti v ulicích' (Comedians in the Streets) festival is an international street theater event celebrating its 14th edition in 2025. Held in Tábor, Czech Republic, it animates Žižkovo náměstí and surrounding streets with a diverse program featuring aerial acrobatics, new circus performances, giant puppets, clown shows, and interactive performances. The festival showcases artists from various countries, including Argentina, Taiwan, Mexico, and the USA, alongside Czech street theater groups. Workshops in juggling, theatrical makeup, and concerts are also part of the vibrant cultural experience.

fields.name: Sztukauliczna
fields.description: The 32nd Festiwal Sztuka Ulicy (Street Art Festival) is a significant open-air theatre event in Central Europe, transforming urban spaces in Warsaw into stages for diverse performances. The 2025 edition featured nine street spectacles, a steampunk parade, an improvised concert, family circus workshops, and various circus and clown acts by artists from Ukraine, Spain, Italy, and Poland. Key performances included 'A Lost Dream' by Teatr Ósmego Dnia, 'The Baltic Story' by Teatr Bazar, and international acts like 'La Bella Tour' by Roi Borralla & Guillermo Balagans (Spain) and 'Boato' by Andy Spigola (Italy). The festival promotes street performance, social activity, and cultural exchange across venues such as New Town Market Square, Szczęśliwicki Park, and Plac Defilad.

fields.name: Berlin Juggling Conv
fields.description: The Berliner Jonglierconvention (Berlin Juggling Convention) is an annual 3-4 day juggling convention typically held in early summer at the FEZ Children's Centre in Berlin. The event is organized by Circulum e.V. and features workshops, performances, and open-stage opportunities for jugglers and circus artists. The 2023 edition took place from May 18–21, and the 2025 dates were pending announcement as of April 30, 2025.

fields.name: Bremen Juggling Con
fields.description: The 8th Internationales Jugend-Zirkus-Festival 'Bridges for Youth' in Bremen, hosted by SVGO-Bremen, features circus groups from Italy, Poland, Belgium, Palestine, and the local 'Zirkus Fiffix.' The festival's motto is 'Boundless Imagination,' with workshops led by professional circus trainers culminating in a joint show. Performances include juggling, aerial acrobatics, partner acrobatics, and unicycling, scheduled for August 3 (Circus Jokes tent), August 5 (SVGO-Halle), and August 9–10 (Shakespeare Company). All shows are free, with donations welcome and advance reservations required due to limited seating.

fields.name: Fuori Asse
fields.description: The Fuori Asse Festival is a contemporary circus event dedicated to new dramaturgies, featuring three days of free outdoor performances, music, and creative workshops. Organized by Quattrox4 in collaboration with Nina's Drag Queens and NAMA – Nuovo Anfiteatro Martesana, the festival is part of the 'Milano è viva' program by the Municipality of Milan.

fields.name: OFCA Festival
fields.description: The 11th edition of the OFCA Festival (Oleśnicki Festiwal Cyrkowo-Artystyczny), a contemporary circus festival featuring street performances, indoor circus-theatre shows by Polish and international artists, and circus workshops for children. The festival also includes areas for adults such as the 'Bar Żongler,' food trucks, chillout music, and slackline activities. Some performances require tickets, available online through the festival's website.

fields.name: Feta Festival
fields.description: The XXVII International Street and Open-Air Theatres Festival FETA in Gdańsk, Poland, will take place from July 10 to 13, 2025, under the theme 'Magic Stories'. This four-day event features theatrical attractions, including street performances, open-air shows, puppet shows, new circus acts, and happenings. The festival kicks off with a grand parade in the city center, followed by performances primarily in Ronald Reagan Park and the Old Town area. Organized by the Gdańsk Archipelago of Culture and the City of Gdańsk, FETA attracts over 30,000 spectators and more than 20 international theater groups annually. The 2025 program includes performances from France, Spain, the UK, Hungary, South Korea, and Poland, with notable Polish groups like Teatr Biuro Podróży, Teatr KTO, and Teatr Klinika Lalek participating. Most events are free, though some may require tickets.

fields.name: Dionysia Theatre Festival
fields.description: The Dionysia Theatre Festival 2025 is an immersive theatre experience featuring outdoor performances, spectacles with large masks, modern interpretations of classics, and moving musical shows. The theme for this year's edition is 'Full immersion in theatre.' Performances are held at the Amphitheater in Dębinka Park, Łochów, with free admission to outdoor events and tickets required only for a specific performance on July 10 in the cinema hall of MiGOK.

fields.name: MFTU
fields.description: The 42nd International Street Theatre Festival of Alina Obidniak (MFTU) in Jelenia Góra, Poland, features performances by international and Polish theatre groups, interactive workshops, and a bone marrow donor registration drive. The festival takes place at Plac Ratuszowy (Town Hall Square) and is organized by the Cyprian Kamil Norwid Theatre.

fields.name: Cyrkopole
fields.description: The 8th edition of Cyrkopole – the Circus Backyard Festival – is an international new circus event held annually as part of the Wrocław City Holiday. The festival showcases a variety of performances by Polish and international artists, including street shows, indoor circus-theatrical spectacles, and activities for children such as a Circus Playground, art workshops, and balancing lessons. Highlights include performances like 'Cyrkowe Variete' by Kolektyw Performatywy, 'NIE-TAK-TY' by Kolektyw KEJOS, and 'ROCOCO!' by Argentinian duo CIA RAMPANTE. The festival emphasizes new circus art, with no animals involved in performances. Most events are free, while some require tickets available through Biletyna.pl or the CBK Fama box office.

fields.name: Ulicznicy
fields.description: The International Street Artists Festival 'Ulicznicy' transforms the streets, parks, and squares of Gliwice into stages for artists from Poland and around the world. The 18th edition features a variety of performances, including theatre, street acts, and premieres, with many events offering free admission. Some performances in the Ruins of Victoria Theatre require free tickets, collectible at specific locations in Gliwice.

fields.name: Busker Tour
fields.description: The Busker Tour 2025 is a travelling street festival of performing and circus arts across multiple cities in Poland. It features over 30 international groups from 20 countries, showcasing acrobatics, juggling, original music, comedy, dance, theatre, magic, clowning, and circus props. The festival is curated by artistic director Małgorzata Węglarz and is known for its original, diverse, and interdisciplinary program. Admission is free, with audiences encouraged to tip the artists.
fields.town: Żary, Krotoszyn, Zielona Góra, Zduńska Wola, Wrocław

fields.name: Kleinkunsttraum Oranienbaum
fields.description: The Kleinkunsttraum Oranienbaum festival transforms the baroque Schlosspark Oranienbaum into a vibrant 'dream of small arts,' featuring over 40 artists performing acrobatics, magic, comedy, clowning, puppetry, and pantomime across 16 stages, along with walking acts. The event includes evening light and laser shows on the castle facade. Creative direction is led by Susanne Linzer from Monstertheater Berlin, curating exceptional artists from across Europe.

fields.name: Pflasterzauber
fields.description: Pflasterzauber is a vibrant outdoor festival in Hildesheim, Germany, transforming city squares into stages for circus, theater, and dance performances. The 18th edition (2025) featured 19 acts from Austria, Spain, France, Italy, and Germany, including stilt dances, acrobatics, clowning, Cyr Wheel, aerial performances, and interactive children's theater. All performances were free, with artists relying on audience donations. The festival is a collaboration between Hildesheim Marketing GmbH and Internationale Kunst- und Kulturprojekte Hildesheim e.V., supported by Stiftung Sparda-Bank Hannover. A digital guide and traditional flyer were available for navigation.

fields.name: Hut Ab! Bielefeld
fields.description: The 'Hut ab! Das Festival der Straßenkunst' transforms Bielefeld's city center into a vibrant stage for street performers, featuring live music, acrobatics, comedy, magic, juggling, and artistry. Over 100 artists perform across 15 locations, including Obernstr./Klasingstr., Bunnemannplatz, Alter Markt, Jahnplatz, and the Loom area. Artists primarily earn through 'Hutgeld' (hat money) collected from the audience. The event also includes Sunday shopping in the city center until 6:00 PM.

fields.name: Strassenkulturfest Nordhorn
fields.description: The 34th International Street Culture Festival 'Nordhorn staunt + lacht' (Nordhorn is amazed + laughs) transforms the city center into a vibrant stage with free outdoor performances ('umsonst + draußen') from 1 PM to 6 PM daily. The festival features international artists, clowns, and magicians, including acts like Fraser Hooper (New Zealand) with 'Funny Business,' manoAmano (Argentina) with pole acrobatics 'NOSO3,' and Jens Ohle (Germany) with artistic comedy. Additionally, ticketed Varieté gala evenings and a family gala are held in a circus tent on Neumarkt.

fields.name: Carnaval Sztukmistrzów
fields.description: Poland's largest festival dedicated to contemporary circus arts, the Carnaval Sztukmistrzów (Festival of Jugglers) in Lublin features a diverse program including street performances, stage shows, workshops, a busker competition, and the Urban Highline Festival. The 2025 edition (16th year) will showcase international acts such as Gabriela Muñoz's clowning show 'Julieta,' My!Laika's surreal 'Winter,' and aerial acrobatics by Art For Rainy Days. Events are held in public spaces like the Old Town and Błonia, with select ticketed performances in venues such as the Old Theatre and circus tents.

fields.name: Guetersloh Strassenfiffi
fields.description: The Gütersloher Strassenfiffi is an annual street art festival held as part of the Michaeliswoche in Gütersloh. The 2025 edition will take place on Sunday, September 28, from 1:00 PM to 6:00 PM in the city center (Berliner Platz and Kolbeplatz/Konrad-Adenauer-Platz). The festival features international street artists performing acrobatics, comedy, juggling, magic, slapstick, dance, improvisational theater, and ladder/unicycle artistry. Admission is free, with artists collecting tips ('Hutgeld') from the audience. Organized by Gütersloh Marketing (gtm) on behalf of Verkehrsverein Gütersloh, the event is part of the broader Michaeliswoche (September 26 – October 5, 2025), which also includes a fair, children's flea market, and shopping Sunday.

fields.name: Letni Letna
fields.description: Letní Letná is an international festival of new circus and theatre held annually in Prague's Letná Gardens. The 22nd edition (2025) runs from August 13 to 31, featuring over 150–200 performances across genres like new circus, theatre, music, and visual arts. The festival includes free open-air shows, workshops for children, and performances by acclaimed international and Czech troupes. The event is recognized as a premier cultural festival in the Czech Republic and neighboring countries, offering accessible programming with free and ticketed events (130–1,250 CZK). The grounds are equipped with relaxation zones, refreshments, and disability access.

fields.name: Ascona Street Festival
fields.description: The **International Street Artists Festival, Ascona** (Festival Artisti di Strada) is a prominent cultural event featuring performances by street artists. The next confirmed edition is scheduled for **May 22–24, 2026**, as per the official Ascona events website. While listed among top events for 2025 by tourism sources, no confirmed dates for that year are available, and conflicting reports suggest potential cancellation or postponement.

fields.name: Internationales Strassentheather Holzminden
fields.description: The International Street Theatre Festival in Holzminden is a biennial event held every two years on the Whitsun weekend since 1991. The festival transforms the town into a vibrant stage for international artist groups and solo performers, featuring acrobatics, clown theater, performance art, street theater, dance, and contemporary circus. The 17th edition (2025) includes 46 performances by eleven artist groups from nine nations, with highlights such as three German premieres, one international premiere, and a dedicated family program. The event attracts around 40,000 visitors and culminates in an audience award ceremony where the public votes for their favorite performances in 'Small Format' and 'Large Format' categories.

fields.name: Bildstoerung Strassentheather Festival Detmold
fields.description: The European Street Theater Festival 'Bildstörung' in Detmold is a biennial event held since 1991, featuring national and international artists across performance, installations, dance, music, and contemporary circus. It aims to contribute to urban society by presenting critical yet entertaining productions in public spaces. The main festival is scheduled for May 22–25, 2026, with a residency program in 2025 including Hippana.Maleta (August 18–20, 2025) and ZWERMERS (2025).

fields.name: Welttheater Der Strasse Schwerte
fields.description: The Welttheater der Straße is an annual international street theater festival held in Schwerte, Germany. Now in its 32nd edition (2025), the festival transforms the city center into a vibrant stage for diverse artistic performances, including theater, acrobatics, contemporary circus, and performative installations. All events are free of charge. Organized by the Kultur- und Weiterbildungsbetrieb (KuWeBe) Schwerte, the festival features premieres, symposia, and collaborations with renowned national and international artists, such as Les P'tits Bras (France) and Circus unARTiq. The 2025 edition runs from August 29 to August 31, with highlights including the opening show 'Vent d'Ouest' and the grand production 'Silence!' by Les Commandos Percu.

fields.name: Sommerwerft Festival
fields.description: The Sommerwerft Festival is an international theater festival held annually in Frankfurt am Main, transforming the Mainufer at Weseler Werft into a vibrant cultural space. The 2025 edition, themed 'Zusammen wachsen' (Growing Together) or 'Wachstum' (Growth), runs from July 25 to August 10, 2025. It features free open-air theater, dance, music, circus performances, and poetry for all ages, with a focus on community, nature, and environmental awareness. Highlights include the 'TREES – How to raise environmental awareness through performing art' sub-festival (July 30–August 3) and the 'MakeEUBlue: Cities in Action' campaign for ocean sustainability. The festival attracts over 100,000 visitors annually, showcasing work from over 200 artists across five stages. Entry is free, with voluntary contributions welcomed.

fields.name: Internationales Straßentheaterfestival Ludwigshafen
fields.description: The International Street Theater Festival Ludwigshafen is an annual three-day event (July 25–27, 2025) transforming the city center into a vibrant stage for international artists. Featuring 18 groups from 13 countries, the festival offers diverse performances—acrobatics, clownish acts, political belly dance, and interactive initiatives—across five uniquely themed venues: *knall, puff, peng* (Karl-Kornmann-Platz), *Piazza Sonata* (Platz der Deutschen Einheit), *Blaues Wunder* (Rheinschanzenpromenade), *Jump & Flow* (Berliner Platz), and *Freibad der Gefühle* (Bismarckstraße). Highlights include anarchic crime parodies, aerial acts, and socially conscious performances like *Limbo* (escape/hope) and *Dancing Queer* (LGBTQ+ empowerment). The *Forever Young* initiative encourages visitor participation, with festival wristbands priced at €5. Main sponsor: Sparkasse Vorderpfalz.

fields.name: Just For Fun
fields.description: The 'Just For Fun' Street Theater Festival in Darmstadt is an annual event that promotes cultural accessibility with free and open performances. The 32nd edition, scheduled for July 30 to August 2, 2025, features contemporary circus, street comedy, acrobatics, and puppet theater, attracting up to 6,000 attendees. The festival also includes the 'Just For Fun Express,' a mobile street theater initiative running from August 8 to August 31, 2025, bringing family-friendly performances to 15 locations across Darmstadt and its surrounding districts via cargo bikes and bicycle trailers.

fields.name: Da Capo Festival
fields.description: The Kronberger Kulturkreis organizes cultural events in Kronberg, including the annual 'Da Capo!' International Street Theater Festival. This free-admission festival transforms the old town into open-air stages featuring acrobats, entertainers, and artists. The 2025 edition marks the 25th anniversary, showcasing beloved returning acts like Jochen the elephant, the 'Living Couch,' and the 'Arbor' children's carousel. A separate ticketed event, 'Das kleine Da Capo,' is held at Kronberg Castle, with free entry for children, teenagers, and apprentices.

fields.name: Herbst Und Weinfest Radebeul
fields.description: The Herbst- und Weinfest Radebeul (Autumn and Wine Festival) is an annual celebration held in Altkötzschenbroda, Radebeul, Germany. The 33rd edition in 2025, themed 'Fire, Water & Trumpets,' takes place from September 19th to 21st. The festival combines wine culture with the 28th International Wandertheaterfestival (Traveling Theater Festival), featuring over 300 artists from 16 nations. Highlights include Saxon wine tastings at 40+ venues, street theater, acrobatics, live music (Latin, salsa, jazz), and family-friendly activities like donkey rides, crafts, and a sing-along choir. The event culminates with a fire and theater spectacle on the Elbe meadows, accompanied by an audience prize ceremony. Public transport is recommended for access.

fields.name: Mimos
fields.description: The Mimos Festival, organized by L'Odyssée Périgueux since 1983, is the most significant festival of its kind in France, dedicated to the arts of gesture, including mime, gestural theatre, bodily performance, dance, circus, object theatre, and puppetry. The 42nd edition (July 1–5, 2025) explores the theme of the 'anthropocene,' featuring performances, discussions, and free/open-access events across Périgueux. The festival includes both paid ('in') and free ('off') programs, with ticketed performances ranging from 8 to 20 euros. Highlights include the opening fire spectacle *Pyromènes* by La Machine, *Encounters in Paradise* (artist-thinker dialogues), and a closing free ball on July 5th.

fields.name: Dresden Mime Festival
fields.description: The International Mime Theatre Festival in Dresden, organized by Mimenstudio Dresden e.V., is a premier event showcasing modern movement theatre. The festival features performances, workshops, and exhibitions by international artists from countries such as Italy, Israel, Czech Republic, Spain, and France. Held annually, the 40th edition (2025) will take place from November 12th to 16th at Theaterhaus Rudi and Projekttheater (Spiegelsaal), with pre-festival workshops starting November 8th. The program includes galas, family-friendly shows, masterclasses, and an exhibition on the history of pantomime in Europe.

fields.name: Festival Of Monodrama And Mime
fields.description: The annual Festival of Monodrama and Mime, established in 1973, is dedicated to showcasing mimes and monodramas. The festival traditionally takes place near the end of June or early July, though recent editions (e.g., the 47th in October 2023 and the 48th in November 2024) have shifted to later in the year. It features performances (max. 60 minutes) at venues like the Puppet Theatre 'Pinocchio' and awards prizes in categories such as best monodrama, best mime, most successful participant, ingenuity, and the Audience's award. Participants receive accommodation, meals, reimbursement, and technical support. The 2025 festival details, including dates and application deadlines, are pending announcement by the organizers.

fields.name: Mim Sueca
fields.description: The Mostra Internacional de Mim a Sueca (MIM Sueca) is an annual festival dedicated to theatrical performances, both indoor and street-based, emphasizing gesture, body movement, and mime as primary artistic and communicative elements. Held in Sueca, Valencia, typically in the third week of September, the festival attracts thousands of attendees of various nationalities and ages. The 2025 edition (35th anniversary) is scheduled from September 18 to 21, featuring a curated program of participating artists and companies.

fields.name: Aufgetischt
fields.description: The 'Aufgetischt' festival is a street art event held annually in St. Gallen, Switzerland. The 12th edition in 2025 (May 9–11) transformed the old town into an open-air stage with over 40 acts from 23 countries, featuring music, acrobatics, dance, and comedy across 400+ shows. The festival also includes a street food market and attracts thousands of visitors. The next edition is scheduled for May 15–17, 2026.

fields.name: Roi De L'Oiseau
fields.description: The Roi de l'Oiseau festival, celebrating its 40th edition in 2025, transports Le Puy-en-Velay back to the Renaissance era with five days of vibrant celebrations, historical reenactments, and cultural immersion. The event features approximately 250 free spectacles, historical camps, artisan demonstrations, and troupes from across Europe, spread over seven hectares of the old town. Highlights include an opening concert by the Italian group Daridel, grand balls, Vellave wrestling, the crowning of the 'King of the Bird' following an archery competition, and the 'Dragon Time' luminous show with fire and pyrotechnics. The festival also hosts one of the largest Renaissance markets in France, attracting around 120,000 visitors who often participate in period costumes.

fields.name: Ostflimmern
fields.description: Ostflimmern is a FlowArts & Fire Convention focused on flow arts, fire dancing, and community experiences. The event features workshops in Hula Hoop, Flowerstick, Acrobatics & Yoga, along with music, healing spaces, camping, and meditations. It fosters a playful, creative, and close-knit atmosphere among approximately 300 participants. The 2025 edition is scheduled for June 6–9, 2025, and is sold out, though a waiting list is available for last-minute openings.

fields.name: British Juggling Convention
fields.description: The British Juggling Convention (BJC) 2026 is a seven-day event scheduled from April 3, 2026, to April 9, 2026, at All Hallows Catholic High School in Penwortham, Preston. The convention features a 24-hour juggling hall, multiple high-ceiling workshop spaces, a central atrium, and outdoor green areas. The event offers a packed program of workshops covering juggling and associated circus skills for all levels, alongside a timetable of shows, including a welcoming show, an open stage, and the 'British Young Juggler of the Year' (BYJOTY) competition. Special guests, including talented jugglers and circus artists, will perform and run workshops. Tickets are already on sale.

fields.name: London Juggling Festival
fields.description: The London Juggling & Flow Arts Festival is a not-for-profit event organized by volunteers, featuring performances, workshops, open practice spaces, and stalls. The 2026 edition will take place from October 22nd to 25th, with evening performances at Jacksons Lane and daytime activities at the National Centre for Circus Arts on October 24th and 25th. The festival actively seeks contributions from jugglers and flow artists, particularly those under 25 and London-based performers.

fields.name: Cultural Centre De Spil (Be)
fields.description: De Spil is a prominent cultural center in Roeselare, Belgium, offering a diverse program across genres including concerts, theater, comedy, dance, film, and exhibitions. The venue features a 844-seat theater, a fly tower, modern technical equipment, a multi-purpose room with retractable seating, and an exhibition space. The 2026 program includes performances by artists such as Bill Frisell, Milow, Tristero, and more, alongside film screenings and the Kunstenfestival Roeselare (January 23–March 22, 2026).

fields.name: Destelheide
fields.description: Destelheide, located in Dworp on the green outskirts of Brussels, Belgium, is a versatile venue offering an 'Artist in Residence' program for creative professionals, including individual artists, theatre/circus/dance/music companies, visual artists, writers, designers, and photographers. The venue provides studios, rehearsal spaces, and accommodation at a subsidized cost (meals and lowest-price overnight stays on a full-board basis). Residencies are flexible in duration, with no minimum/maximum requirement, and include technical support for equipment setup. Artists can also organize try-outs, performances, or open rehearsals for guests (typically aged 16+). In 2026, Destelheide will host a 'Creative Education Assistant' volunteer role (Jan 19–May 19) and European Solidarity Corps volunteers, emphasizing cultural exchange.

fields.name: Espace Catastrophe
fields.description: UP - Circus & Performing Arts (formerly Espace Catastrophe) is the International Creation Centre for Circus Arts, located in Molenbeek-Saint-Jean, Brussels. Starting in 2025, it will occupy a new 6,000m² permanent venue at Rue Osseghem 50, 1080 Molenbeek-Saint-Jean, Belgium, dedicated to creation, performance, training, and residencies for circus arts. It is closely associated with the annual UP Festival, which takes place from March 19 to March 29, 2026, featuring contemporary circus performances across Brussels.

fields.name: Theatre Denamur
fields.description: The Théâtre de Namur is an active and historic venue in Namur, Belgium, recognized as one of Europe's most beautiful 19th-century Italian-style theatres. Reconstructed in 1863 and fully renovated in 1998, it features sophisticated modern scenic techniques. The theatre offers a diverse program including plays, concerts, dance shows, circus performances, and workshops, often in collaboration with the Centre culturel de Namur (CCN). The 2025-2026 season includes performances such as *Rumba* by Ascanio Celestini, *Crime et châtiment* by Compagnie Karyatides, *Maison Chaos* by Joëlle Sambi, and *L'appel des mutantes* by Lucile Charnier, among others.

fields.name: The Halls Of Schaerbeel
fields.description: Les Halles de Schaerbeek is a cultural venue in Belgium offering a diverse program of events across dance, theater, circus, and music. Its missions include supporting artists, engaging with the local community in Schaerbeek and Brussels, and addressing contemporary societal issues such as climate, migration, gender, and social justice. The programming is organized into thematic blocks: artistic focus from October to February, musical emphasis from February to May, and a season-opening program in September.

fields.name: Maison De La Culture De Tournai
fields.description: The Maison de la Culture de Tournai is a recognized cultural center and scenic venue, subsidized by the Wallonia-Brussels Federation and the City of Tournai. It serves as a space for cultural diffusion and creative expression, hosting performances, exhibitions, and events. The venue features a modular stage with 300 seats, accommodating both seated and standing concerts. The box office is open Tuesday to Friday (1 PM–6 PM) and Saturdays (9 AM–1 PM).

fields.name: Municipal Theater Sint-Niklaas
fields.description: CC Sint-Niklaas (Stadsschouwburg Sint-Niklaas) is a municipal theatre in Sint-Niklaas, Belgium, hosting a diverse program of contemporary theatre, live music, dance, circus, and family shows. The venue also features exhibitions and intimate events across multiple locations, including the CC Museumtheater at Zwijgershoek, De Foyer, and the Tentoonstellingszaal Zwijgershoek. Recently renovated for improved accessibility, it is known for its quality performances and comfortable seating. The 2025-2026 season includes performances by artists such as Urbanus, Günther Lesage, and Compagnie Cecilia, alongside festivals like 'De Nacht'.

fields.name: Perplx
fields.description: Perplx is a cultural organization based in Kortrijk, Belgium, organizing yearly outdoor festivals during the last weekend of June or the first weekend of July, and indoor festivals in January in collaboration with the local cultural center. They also host 'Soirée Perplx' events in March and April, typically at venues like Budascoop, Budatoren, or Unie der zorgelozen.

fields.name: Y Not Art Loft
fields.description: Y'Not Art-Loft is an active venue in Berlin's industrial area of Georg-Knorr Park (Marzahn), offering 270m² across three rooms for events like corporate functions, photoshoots, film sets, meetings, and private parties. Operated by Cosmic Artists, it specializes in acrobatic and artistic show concepts and provides a versatile, barrier-free space with ample parking and proximity to public transport (S-Bahn and tram).

fields.name: Zirkus Mond
fields.description: Zirkus Mond is a contemporary circus, theater, and event venue in Berlin-Prenzlauer Berg, serving as a meeting point for the city's artist scene. The venue hosts circus, theater, dance, and concerts, operating from a rented fallow land shared with other collectives. It is scheduled to open at Lilli-Henoch-Straße 10, 10405 Berlin, in 2026, with events including a New Year's Eve gala to welcome 2026.

fields.name: Callie'S
fields.description: Callie's is a non-profit experimental institution in Berlin dedicated to fostering creativity and cultural exchange through residencies, exhibitions, and public programs. The residency program is self-guided, offering artists private studio spaces (with natural light, alarm systems, heat, and WiFi) and three micro-apartments for visiting artists. Residencies typically last six months, though shorter stays can be requested. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, with selection based on artistic fit, complementarity of practices, and application strength. As of January 2026, applications are currently closed.

fields.name: Fleestreet Theater
fields.description: The Fleetstreet Theater in Hamburg is a residence theater for artist groups, offering space and time for research, experimentation, and production across disciplines including theater, performance, dance, visual arts, music, and film. The venue combines stage and gallery space, accommodating smaller productions with a seating capacity of approximately 60-80 and a stage measuring 4 x 6 meters. While not maintaining a continuous public performance schedule, it hosts presentations by resident artists and its 'DramaTisch' series of staged readings. A notable upcoming event in 2026 is 'Quick Fix' by HERMANN / Joelina Spiess, Fran Vozila, David Knap, Charlie Smeets, and Hülya Braasch, scheduled for January 23 and 24, 2026, at 20:00.

fields.name: Dublin Circus Project
fields.description: The Dublin Circus Project offers 'Catalyst' and 'Ignition' residency programs for emerging, mid-career, and established artists, in collaboration with Dance Ireland and supported by the Arts Council. The organization operates from the Dublin Circus Centre in Cherry Orchard, Dublin 10, providing a dedicated training space with equipment and props for circus and multidisciplinary artists.

fields.name: Theatre Louis Aragon
fields.description: The Théâtre Louis Aragon is a 'scène conventionnée d'intérêt national Art et création - danse' located in Tremblay-en-France, France. It is supported by the City of Tremblay-en-France, the Department of Seine-Saint-Denis, the Île-de-France Region, and the Ministry of Culture. The theatre offers a multidisciplinary program featuring theatre, dance, circus, and music, with a seating capacity of approximately 450 places.

fields.name: les aires theater de die et du diois
104 Paris
fields.description: Le Centquatre (104) is a prominent public cultural center in the 19th arrondissement of Paris, established in October 2008. It serves as a multidisciplinary art space dedicated to artistic residencies, production, and showcasing theatre, dance, circus, music, visual arts, and performances. The venue hosts exhibitions, workshops, community activities, and offers studio spaces for artists in residence, open public areas for artistic engagement, shops, restaurants, and 'La Maison des Petits,' a dedicated space for children aged 0 to 6.

fields.name: 2R2C
fields.description: 2r2c (La Coopérative De Rue et De Cirque) operates primarily at **RueWATT**, a performance space in Paris offering open artistic residencies from March 9, 2026, to December 18, 2026. Facilities include a 100 m² rehearsal hall with a 7-meter ceiling, sound system, dressing rooms, and a shared kitchen. Additionally, 2r2c organizes the **Village de Cirque**, an annual contemporary circus festival held in October at Big Tops in **la Pelouse de Reuilly Park**, Paris.

fields.description: Le Jardin Parallèle is a cultural center dedicated to the art of puppetry and associated arts, located in Reims, France. It hosts the annual Orbis Pictus Festival, featuring performances and exhibitions in historic venues across the Forum district, including the Cryptoportique, Hôtel de la Salle, Musée-Hôtel le Vergeur, and Le Cellier.

fields.short_description: Ah Bah Bravo (French for 'well done then!') is an interactive, funny, and light-hearted contemporary circus show, ideal for family audiences. Created and performed by Philippe Ducasse, the piece can be adapted between 20 and 45 minutes and is designed for street and open-air performances.
fields.email_prompt:
Dear [festivalName] Team,

I am Philippe Ducasse, a circus artist based in Berlin with a passion for blending juggling, mime, and clowning into vibrant, family-friendly performances. I would like to propose my show "Ah Bah Bravo!" to the next edition of your festival.

"Ah Bah Bravo!" is a whirlwind of world-clss juggling, including butt hooping, flaming staff juggling while handstanding, and playful storytelling. It's a joyful celebration of childhood wonder, inviting audiences to laugh, dream, and embrace the magic of the moment.

Here you can see the <a href="https://example.com/trailer">trailer</a>

The dossier is attached with full details, photos, and technical requirements.

Looking forward to your reply!
fields.long_description:
A wonderful mix of juggling, acrobatics, and mime, Ah Bah Bravo! is a poetic celebration of these diverse disciplines, presented with a good dose of humor and joie de vivre. The show includes world-class juggling - spinning a flaming staff with the feet in a handstand, butt hopping, and much, much more!
It is also a journey into the world of childhood, where spectators, young and old, have the opportunity to share and relive certain aspects of this magical phase of life: an imaginary car ride, discovering oneself for the first time in a mirror, and lots and lots of silliness. This is a story that celebrates the carefree spirit of childhood, inviting viewers to dream, to share, and above all, not to take themselves too seriously.

fields.short_description: Lulu is a 45-minute contemporary circus piece created and performed by Philippe. It tells the story of a troubled individual desperately searching for the solution to all of his problems - 'Lulu'. The varied piece includes mime, object manipulation, acrobatics, and dance - as well as strong use of dark humour.
fields.long_description:
He is searching. constantly searching, for Lulu.
In parallel to his desperate search is an equally desperate escape. He is running
away; from his fears, his desires, his reality.
Lulu is the story of this paradoxical quest, which leads a troubled, nameless protagonist through deserts, skies, Alan Watts lectures and strip clubs. No matter how far he runs, the path always seems to take him back to where he started. He is constantly brought back to the misery of his reality, and
confronted with his own self.
Lulu explores sensitive themes such as loneliness, isolation, and substance abuse, and turns them on their head by asking the audience to see comedy in tragedy. A central theme of Lulu is how beautifully absurd suffering sometimes is, and how being able to be curious about and even laugh at our most painful experiences makes them much easier to bear.

Mi chiamo Philippe Ducasse, artista di circo. Vorrei proporre il mio spettacolo "Ah Bah Bravo!" per la prossima edizione de La Luna nel Pozzo.

"Ah Bah Bravo!" è un’esplosione di giocoleria mozzafiato: staffe infiocate in verticale con i piedi, butt hopping, e il tutto avvolto in una narrazione tenera e divertente. Uno spettacolo che trasforma le strade in un palcoscenico di meraviglia, perfetto per il vostro festival che celebra l’arte di strada in tutte le sue forme: acrobatica, clownesca e sognatrice. Un viaggio nel mondo dell’infanzia, dove grandi e piccini ridono, si emozionano e riscoprono la leggerezza di giocare senza regole.
